  change. Separate chore/activities-log commit (this entry) follows the
  Session 24 pre-work convention.

    prop for Phase C deep-link

  Frontend defense (priority low, bundle with admin-backend-500s):

      spots don't check response.ok before passing .json() to state

       refactor filed as chore/resources-derive-state-from-prop.

  Orphaned files on disk (decision deferred):

      both the UI and the manifest (Download Markdown removed). Kept on
      disk intentionally; pruning is a Phase C (or later) decision.

      safety audit and the Resources shape decisions cleanly. Pause 2b's
      Download Markdown correction surfaced because of the real-UI click-
      test gate between commits 2 and 3. Pause 3's clean separation of
      commit 5 enabled accurate attribution of the Admin → Staff blank-
      screen crash as a pre-existing backend issue, not a regression
      from the view-state collapse. Pause discipline and commit

SESSION: 2026-04-23T12:59:00-07:00
BRANCH:  fix/parent-portal-intervention-write-access
DISPOSITION: pr-opened:https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/8
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TASK SUMMARY
  Parent-portal intervention-write-access bug fix. Before this branch,
  parents viewing the portal could POST/PUT/DELETE on /api/weekly-progress
  for any intervention (including staff-assigned ones), and
  GET /api/interventions/student/:studentId was unauthenticated. Branch
  adds a new shared middleware with role-branched authorization on both
  the write endpoints and the intervention read endpoint, and hides UI
  affordances parents should not use. 7 commits, PR #8 opened, all three
  reviewers returned APPROVED WITH NOTES, both pre-merge WARNs addressed.

FILES TOUCHED
  middleware/authorizeInterventionAccess.js           new — requireAuth + 3 role-branched gates
  routes/weeklyProgress.js                            gated POST/PUT/DELETE; forced logged_by+student_id server-side
  routes/interventions.js                             gated GET /student/:studentId; added current_user_can_log flag; 500 generic
  frontend/src/App.jsx                                ParentPortalView gate on "Log Progress"; credentials:'include' on 5 fetches; Lock icon import
  docs/ai-context/PRIVACY_REVIEW.md                   new "Approved data-minimization exceptions" section

MIGRATIONS RUN
  migration-013-password-reset.sql (local only)       pre-existing drift from main; applied to local DB to unblock test-account creation. Not introduced by this branch.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  Every new/modified SQL query verified by tenant-isolation-auditor.
  Authoritative tenant lookup pattern: for writes, derived from
  student_interventions -> students.tenant_id via server-side join; for
  reads, students.tenant_id fetched directly. Parent branch gated by
  parent_student_links (reads) and intervention_assignments (writes).
  Non-parent branch gated by tenant match. No cross-tenant read or write
  path introduced. Verdict: APPROVED WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched: none newly persisted. Existing student_interventions
    "notes" field is now intentionally readable by role=parent callers
    (documented exception, see PRIVACY_REVIEW.md section added in Commit 7).
  Logging audit: 6 new console.error sites log only err.message prefixed
    with a static tag. No student/staff/tenant identifiers interpolated.
  Error responses: all 403/401 paths use generic bodies
    ({"error":"Not authorized"} / {"error":"Not authenticated"}).
    Raw pg error leak on GET /interventions/student/:studentId fixed in
    Commit 6.
  Cross-tenant risk: none identified. Privacy-reviewer verdict:
    APPROVED WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL. Both pre-merge WARNs addressed in
    Commits 6 and 7.

DB FIXTURES CREATED DURING MANUAL TESTING (local DB only, no PII inlined)
  users                       +1 row (role=parent, Lincoln Elementary tenant, bcrypt pw set)
  parent_student_links        +1 row (linking the test parent to one Lincoln student)
  intervention_assignments    +1 row (assignment_type='parent', can_log_progress=TRUE) for one of that student's interventions
  weekly_progress             +1 row via the test write path (logged_by correctly attributed to the test parent)

VERIFICATION
  git status                                clean, working tree matches origin at df1c05f
  node -c (3 touched backend files)         all parse OK
  npm run build (frontend/)                 built in 2.86s, no errors
  npm run lint (frontend/)                  89 pre-existing issues across the repo; 0 on lines this branch touched (grep-verified)
  Manual Tests 1-5 (parent portal flow)     all PASSED on local dev (Ethan Jackson / Individual Counseling / Log Progress gating / edge case can_log_progress=FALSE toggle + revert)
  Security, tenant-isolation, privacy       all 3 reviewers returned APPROVED WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL
  Pre-merge WARN asks                       security WARN 2 -> Commit 5 (86bb4d0); privacy WARN 1 -> Commit 6 (dc3b5e1); privacy WARN 2 -> Commit 7 (df1c05f)

BRANCH CLEANUP DURING CLOSE
  fix/resource-1-4-calendar-underscores   not present locally (already cleaned up)
  chore/activities-log                    not present locally (already cleaned up)
  Only the PR branch and main remain locally; no deletes performed.

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS
  1. sec/require-auth-on-unprotected-routes
       Systematic auth middleware + algorithms:['HS256'] on jwt.verify
       across all route files currently missing JWT enforcement. Elevated
       priority: the 4 GET routes in routes/weeklyProgress.js
       (/student/:studentId, /intervention/:interventionId,
       /summary/:studentId, /missing/:tenantId) surface PII and are now
       easier to exploit from a compromised parent cookie after this
       PR's credentials:'include' change. Flagged by both
       tenant-isolation-auditor and security-reviewer. First milestone
       suggested: sec/weekly-progress-read-scoping. Middleware already
       exists; one-line-per-route application.
  2. Schema drift on parent creation flow (combined cleanup)
       Three related pre-existing bugs surfaced during test-fixture
       setup: (a) missing password_reset_token / password_reset_expires
       columns (fixed locally via migration-013-password-reset.sql; still
       pending on any environment that never ran the migration);
       (b) password_hash NOT NULL constraint violated by the
       parent-creation UI code path; (c) parent_student_links.tenant_id
       referenced by INSERT at routes/auth.js:341-344 but the column is
       missing from schema. All three block parent-creation via the UI.
  3. Dead code
       Duplicate router.put('/:id') handler at
       routes/weeklyProgress.js:294. Unreachable today (first handler
       wins) but a footgun for future refactors. Security-reviewer INFO.
  4. Intervention-assignment model inconsistency
       intervention_assignments is effectively parent-only in practice
       today. Staff authorization flows through tenant scope + admin
       gatekeeping at intervention creation, not explicit assignment
       rows. Whether to consolidate staff onto explicit assignment rows
       is a product decision, not a bug.
  5. Missing success toast on staff weekly progress save
       Writes land in DB with logged_by correctly attributed to the staff
       user, but no UI confirmation appears after successful submit.
       Pre-existing; not introduced here. Candidate for a small chore/.
  6. apiFetch helper refactor (chore/api-fetch-helper)
       Five fetch sites in ParentPortalView all missed
       credentials:'include'; three were active bugs fixed in Commit 4.
       "Five call sites all missed the same thing" is the canonical sign
       the abstraction is missing. Wrap fetch() and default
       credentials:'include', removing the footgun.
  7. Eight additional { error: error.message } leaks in
       routes/interventions.js on pre-existing unauthenticated handlers
       (lines 57, 79, 102, 123, 150, 233, 261, 288). Not reachable to
       authenticated attackers today; become reachable the moment
       sec/require-auth-on-unprotected-routes gates them. Fix alongside
       the auth-gating work.
  8. No rate limiting anywhere in the repo
       Security-reviewer INFO. No express-rate-limit import found.
       Candidate for a separate sec/* followup; lower priority than 1-7.
  9. No 401 interceptor on the frontend
       Privacy-reviewer flagged during Commit 5 grep-verification: users
       see errors on session expiry instead of being redirected to
       login. Pre-existing. Candidate for the sec/ session.
  10. "Rachel Adams" display name discrepancy
       Admin UI shows "Rachel Adams" as the school admin for Lincoln
       Elementary, but no user matches that name by
       users.full_name ILIKE. Lincoln's two admin users are id 6 and
       id 7 by email. Display name sourced from somewhere other than
       users.full_name. Minor investigation; not blocking.

NEXT SESSION SHOULD
  Wait for PR #8 human review + merge before starting sec/ work.
  If the next session is sec/, begin with followup 1 above; that
  session is also the natural home for followups 7, 8, 9 and the
  algorithms:['HS256'] jwt.verify pinning deferred from security WARN 1.

SESSION: 2026-04-23T18:46:20-07:00
BRANCH:  fix/mtss-report-rendering (merged as PR #10 → 5ac69f8 + bba7234, merge commit 0a6cfea)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TASK SUMMARY
  MTSS Progress Report rendering fix. Two bugs in the report modal:
  (1) every date field in the report body rendered as "Invalid Date"
  while the header rendered correctly; (2) a 6-Week Review meeting
  inside the selected date range did not appear in the report's
  meeting-notes section. Pure frontend fix plus a docs update to the
  PII inventory. 2 commits, PR #10 merged with --no-ff preserving
  both SHAs on main. Live deploy verified on production sandbox.

FILES TOUCHED
  frontend/src/components/Modals/ReportModal.jsx    formatDateLocal helper; 4 date call sites routed through it; progress_notes→progressNotes camelCase fix; new authenticated fetch to /mtss-meetings/student/:id; dual-source meeting-notes render (modern mtss_meetings + legacy progressNotes)
  docs/ai-context/PRIVACY_REVIEW.md                 recorded mtss_meetings render surface in the FERPA section (meeting_type, progress_summary, next_steps, created_by_name) per privacy-reviewer WARN

MIGRATIONS RUN
  none

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  No new SQL queries, no new backend handlers, no new tables. Skipped
  tenant-isolation-auditor by design: the endpoint this PR newly calls
  from the frontend (GET /mtss-meetings/student/:studentId) has a
  pre-existing tenant-scoping + auth-middleware gap that is already
  captured as master-index Followup 57 and is out of scope for a
  frontend rendering fix. The new client-side call correctly sends
  credentials:'include', which is a prerequisite for the eventual
  server-side fix and does not worsen the existing gap.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched: four mtss_meetings fields are now rendered in
    the MTSS Progress Report UI — meeting_type, progress_summary,
    next_steps, created_by_name. All were already stored server-side
    pre-PR; this is a new render site, not a new data surface. Added
    to the FERPA inventory in PRIVACY_REVIEW.md (commit bba7234).
  Logging audit: one new logError call on a failed meetings fetch
    logs a static tag plus the raw err object only — no PII fields
    interpolated. logError resolves to console.error.
  Error responses: frontend-only change, no res.json / res.send
    surfaces modified. Non-OK fetch responses fail silently to an
    empty meetings array (no error body surfaced to the user).
  Cross-tenant risk: none introduced by this PR. Privacy-reviewer
    verdict APPROVED WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL, 1 WARN (doc ask
    addressed in commit bba7234), 2 INFO. Security-reviewer verdict
    APPROVED WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL, 0 WARN, 3 INFO.

    GET /mtss-meetings/student/:studentId has no auth middleware and

SESSION: 2026-04-24T08:17:58-07:00
BRANCH:  sec/student-documents-auth (merged as PR #12 → 44abc99 + 8ad78ee + 6f1ff09 + a5845f9 + 69a4ccd, merge commit e0ade82)
DISPOSITION: merged
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TASK SUMMARY
  Student-documents auth hardening + two reported parent-portal bugs.
  User reported "I cannot delete uploaded documents" (Bug A) and
  "download → blank screen on my own uploads" (Bug B), both logged in
  as a test parent. Read-only audit surfaced that routes/studentDocuments.js
  had a forgeable x-user-role HTTP-header RBAC check on DELETE, no auth
  middleware anywhere in the file, no tenant_id scoping on any query,
  and client-supplied tenant_id/uploaded_by on POST /upload. User
  approved Model B ("parents can delete/download their own uploads")
  and a full sec/ rework rather than a narrow fix. 5 commits, PR #12
  merged --no-ff preserving all SHAs on main. Live deploy verified on
  production sandbox.

FILES TOUCHED
  middleware/authorizeDocumentAccess.js   new — requireAuth + 5 resource-scoped authorizers mirroring PR #8's authorizeInterventionAccess.js
  routes/studentDocuments.js              gated all 5 endpoints with real auth; removed forgeable x-user-role header; server-derived tenant_id/student_id/uploaded_by on upload; S3 key rebuilt from server-derived values; generic 500 bodies; err.message-only logs
  frontend/src/App.jsx                    fixed parent data.downloadUrl→data.url typo (Bug B); added handleChildDocumentDelete + conditional Delete button on parent portal (Bug A, Model B); staff + parent upload handlers stop sending tenant_id and uploaded_by

MIGRATIONS RUN
  none

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  Every SQL query in routes/studentDocuments.js now scoped by
  tenant_id using a server-derived value (req.user.tenant_id or
  req.targetStudent.tenant_id resolved in middleware from the
  students row). DELETE /:id repeats AND tenant_id = $2 in SQL as
  defense in depth. GET /expiring/:tenantId sources tenant from
  req.user.tenant_id and joins s.tenant_id = sd.tenant_id as defense
  in depth. POST /upload rebuilds S3 key from server-derived
  tenant+student. Parent branch gated by parent_student_links for
  reads + writes + uploads. Every prior cross-tenant hole in the PR
  description closed. Tenant-isolation-auditor verdict: APPROVED
  WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL, 2 WARN (both defense-in-depth/schema
  hardening suggestions for future work, captured below).

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched: all columns on student_documents
    (file_name, file_url, s3_key, document_category, description,
    expiration_date, uploaded_by). Education records under FERPA.
    No new columns, no new JSON response fields, no new form inputs.
    This PR only changes who can read/write existing fields.
  Logging audit: 11 console.error sites across middleware + routes
    reviewed. All use err.message / error.message only. No PII
    (no student name, student_id, file_name, tenant_id, uploaded_by
    values) interpolated anywhere. Middleware uses [tag] err.message
    pattern matching authorizeInterventionAccess.js.
  Error responses: all five 500 bodies now return
    { error: 'Server error' }. Auth failures return
    { error: 'Not authenticated' } or { error: 'Not authorized' }.
    Legitimate 400 'No file uploaded' preserved. Raw pg error leak
    fully removed on this surface.
  Cross-tenant risk: none introduced; multiple pre-existing
    cross-tenant paths closed. Privacy-reviewer verdict: APPROVED
    WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL, 1 WARN (FERPA inventory in
    PRIVACY_REVIEW.md — deferred to a docs commit per project
    convention, held below), 3 INFO. Security-reviewer verdict:
    APPROVED WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL, 2 WARN (both pre-existing
    posture items, held below), 4 INFO.
  Parent delete of own upload: permanently removes S3 object + DB
    row. Privacy-reviewer flagged as INFO (product-level decision
    rather than privacy-rule violation) since scope is limited to
    docs the parent contributed themselves, not staff-created
    education records.

VERIFICATION
  Read-only audit of routes/studentDocuments.js + parent portal     surfaced forgeable x-user-role RBAC + no auth middleware + no tenant scoping + client-supplied tenant_id/uploaded_by on upload
  node -c (middleware + routes/studentDocuments.js)                 both parse OK across all 5 commits
  npm run build (frontend/)                                         built in 2.84s, no errors
  Privacy-reviewer                                                  APPROVED WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL, 1 WARN (docs deferral, non-blocking), 3 INFO
  Security-reviewer                                                 APPROVED WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL, 2 WARN (both pre-existing posture, non-blocking), 4 INFO
  Tenant-isolation-auditor                                          APPROVED WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL, 2 WARN (defense-in-depth + future schema, non-blocking), 3 INFO
  Live deploy verification (production sandbox)                     Bug A (parent delete) and Bug B (parent download blank screen) both confirmed fixed end-to-end; no visible regressions
  Option B selected to hold all four non-blocking WARNs             no in-branch commit 6; all four WARNs tracked in OPEN ITEMS below

BRANCH CLEANUP DURING CLOSE
  sec/student-documents-auth                left intact locally and on origin; cleanup deferred to the next session's handshake (Session 25/26 convention)

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS
  - [held from PR body] requireAuth is duplicated verbatim between
    middleware/authorizeInterventionAccess.js (Session 25) and
    middleware/authorizeDocumentAccess.js (this PR). Intentional
    DRY debt to keep each resource-scoped authorizer independently
    reviewable. Target: chore/ branch extracting requireAuth into a
    shared middleware/requireAuth.js module and updating both
    resource files to import from it. No behavior change.

  - [held from PR body; security WARN 1] routes/studentDocuments.js
    POST /upload middleware order is requireAuth → multer →
    requireDocumentUploadAccess. Authenticated-but-unauthorized
    callers buffer up to 25 MB in memory before the authorizer
    rejects them. Low-privileged accounts can spam the route to
    force repeated 25 MB allocations and get 403. No rate limiter
    is bound to this route (project-wide gap). Target: sec/ branch
    adding a busboy-level student_id peek before multer, or
    express-rate-limit on the upload route, or both.

  - [held from PR body + privacy WARN 1] docs/ai-context/PRIVACY_REVIEW.md
    FERPA inventory does not yet declare student_documents as a PII
    surface. Deferred to a follow-on docs commit per the precedent
    already set in that file. When the doc surface is declared,
    also record the Parent-delete-of-own-upload retention posture
    (per privacy-reviewer INFO 2).

  - [new; tenant-auditor WARN 1] routes/studentDocuments.js
    GET /student/:studentId SELECT is not join-bound to
    students.tenant_id. Functionally safe today (middleware resolves
    the student's tenant + the SELECT has AND sd.tenant_id = $2)
    but asymmetric with the defense-in-depth JOIN the same PR
    added to GET /expiring. Target: small chore/ branch adding
    JOIN students s ON sd.student_id = s.id AND s.tenant_id = sd.tenant_id
    to both parent and staff branches of the SELECT.

  - [new; security WARN 2] jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET)
    is called without an algorithms allowlist at 6 call sites:
    routes/auth.js:166, 294, 572; routes/tier1-assessments.js:47;
    middleware/authorizeInterventionAccess.js:24;
    middleware/authorizeDocumentAccess.js:41. Residual
    algorithm-confusion exposure. Target: single sec/ sweep pinning
    every jwt.verify to { algorithms: ['HS256'] } plus a paired
    signing-side audit. Related master-index Followup 57
    (sec/require-auth-on-unprotected-routes) — this is a natural
    second commit on that branch.

  - [new; tenant-auditor WARN 2] student_documents has independent
    FKs on tenant_id and student_id, not a composite FK. Nothing at
    the schema layer forces student_documents.tenant_id to equal
    the referenced student's tenant_id; every row-level constraint
    is enforced in application code. Target: future migration/
    branch making students have UNIQUE (id, tenant_id) and changing
    the student_documents FK to (student_id, tenant_id) REFERENCES
    students(id, tenant_id). Makes cross-tenant drift impossible at
    the DB layer. Larger blast radius than the other followups.

  - [new; privacy INFO 3 — minor] routes/studentDocuments.js
    SELECT sd.* on GET /student/:studentId and GET /expiring
    returns student_documents.file_url, which is the raw S3 HTTPS
    URL written at upload time. Since download is presigned-only,
    this column is unused by the client. Candidate for a chore/
    branch that either stops selecting it from list responses or
    deprecates the column entirely. Minor data-minimization
    cleanup, lowest priority of the six.

NEXT SESSION SHOULD
  Open the next session by cleaning up sec/student-documents-auth
  (local + origin) during the handshake, matching the Session 25
  and 26 opening pattern. If the next session targets master-index
  Followup 57 (sec/require-auth-on-unprotected-routes), followups
  2 (multer-before-authorizer), 4 (symmetric JOIN defense in depth),
  and 5 (jwt.verify algorithms allowlist) are its natural first
  commits alongside the Session 25 auth-hardening scope.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  Every modified SELECT scoped by tenant_id using a server-derived
  value (req.user.tenant_id for staff, req.student.tenant_id /
  req.intervention.tenant_id resolved by middleware for parents).
  /missing/:tenantId now sources tenant from req.user.tenant_id;
  the URL path param is consumed only by requireTenantStaffAccess
  for the equality check and never reaches the SQL. Tenant-isolation
  auditor verdict: APPROVED, 0 CRITICAL, 0 WARN, 3 INFO. Specifically
  verified airtight: the parent IDOR scenario (linked to one student,
  passes :interventionId belonging to another) — middleware resolves
  the intervention's actual student first, then checks
  parent_student_links against THAT student id, never against caller-
  supplied data. Returns 403, no bypass path. Also: dynamic param
  indexing shift from $2 to $3 on the optional ?interventionId,
  ?startDate, ?endDate filters verified clean (no off-by-one).

DISPOSITION: all merged

TASK SUMMARY
  Four-PR session anchored on PR #16 (in-modal weekly_progress
  visibility + immutable snapshot persistence on MTSS review
  meetings) plus three small follow-on fixes that surfaced during
  PR #16 production smoke and adjacent tidy-up. PR #16 added a
  weekly_progress_snapshot JSONB column to mtss_meeting_interventions
  via Migration 020, persisted snapshots on POST/PUT, exposed them
  on both GET endpoints, rendered sparkline + expandable disclosure
  + zero-data warning on intervention cards in the meeting form
  modal, and rendered the same data via a three-state discriminator
  on the view-past-meeting block. Pre-PR, saved meetings stored
  only per-intervention aggregates; subsequent edits to or deletions
  of underlying weekly_progress rows silently rewrote what the
  meeting appeared to have reviewed. Post-PR, the meeting record is
  a durable artifact suitable for parent conferences, OCR responses,
  and IEP transitions, and the snapshot pattern is foundational for
  upcoming 504 review meetings (Phase 2+ of the 504 v1 work scoped
  at this session's close).
  PR #17 fixed a print-mode disclosure rendering bug introduced by
  PR #16 commit 1485fc5: the original implementation gated
  visibility on a React isPrinting state flipped by beforeprint /
  afterprint listeners, but window.print() runs synchronously and
  React state batching is async — the print preview captured the
  DOM before the re-render completed, so disclosures never appeared
  on paper. Replaced React-state gating with CSS @media print
  visibility, removing the race entirely.
  PR #18 fixed an "Invalid Date" rendering bug on the MTSS Meeting
  Summary header. Same bug class as Session 26 PR #10 (concatenating
  'T00:00:00' onto a value pg had already serialized as a full ISO
  string with embedded T). Component-scoped formatMeetingDate helper
  matching the established formatDateLocal pattern; two render
  sites updated; truthy guard preserved for the null path.
  PR #19 closed master-index Followup 78: three single-line edits
  in routes/weeklyProgress.js POST/PUT/DELETE catch handlers
  switching err → err.message, mirroring the GET-side pattern that
  Session 28 PR #14 commit 964953a established on the same file.
  End-to-end err.message hygiene on routes/weeklyProgress.js
  complete after this commit.

FILES TOUCHED
  PR #16 (11 commits)

  Migration 020 — additive ALTER TABLE on mtss_meeting_interventions adding
    weekly_progress_snapshot JSONB DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb. Idempotent via ADD
    COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS. Existing rows backfill to '[]'::jsonb via the

    via Render auto-deploy after PR #16 merge.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  PR #16 introduces no new auth gating on routes/mtssMeetings.js (pre-
  existing master-index Followup 67 — no requireAuth on any handler in
  this file). The new computeWeeklyProgressSnapshot SELECT joins
  weekly_progress → student_interventions → students with
  s.tenant_id = $bodyTenantId as DEFENSE IN DEPTH ONLY. Sloppy forgery
  (correct intervention id + wrong tenant) fails closed and returns an
  empty snapshot; targeted forgery (correct intervention id + correct
  tenant) still bypasses because the route lacks requireAuth.
  Tenant-isolation-auditor verdict on PR #16: APPROVED WITH NOTES,
  0 CRITICAL, 3 WARN (all carryover from Followup 67 / 74 / 76; concurs
  with privacy on body.tenant_id JOIN-bind framing), 3 INFO. The auditor
  explicitly verified the new PUT body.tenant_id dependency does not
  create additional isolation surface beyond POST's existing reliance on
  the same field. Snapshot widens what an unauthenticated caller can read
  on /mtss-meetings/* GETs from per-intervention aggregates to individual
  log entries (per-week rating, free-text notes, denormalized logged_by
  name + role) — a meaningful posture-shift that meaningfully raises the
  urgency of closing Followup 67 in the next sec/ branch.
  PRs #17, #18, #19 do not modify SQL; tenant isolation posture
  unchanged.

    index Followups 74 + 76 in a future migration branch. Use
    CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY in production to avoid brief write
    lock. (Lesson applied prophylactically to the 504 v1 schema in
    Session 30 PR #20 Migration 021.)

  - [new; PR #16 security-reviewer INFO 4]
    Followup 82. No server-side cap on weekly_progress.notes length
    or on overall snapshot row size. JSONB column is uncapped at the
    schema level; a school with chatty staff could push individual
    snapshots well past the ~7.5 KB worst-case estimate. Bundle with
    Followup 81 or stand alone depending on future migration scope.

  - [held; raised in urgency by PR #16 posture-shift]
    Master-index Followup 67. routes/mtssMeetings.js has no
    requireAuth middleware on any handler. PR #16's snapshot column
    widens the unauthenticated read surface from per-intervention
    aggregates to individual log entries with staff attribution and
    free-text notes. Both privacy-reviewer and tenant-isolation-
    auditor explicitly recorded the urgency raise. Fix shape (per
    auditor concurrence): requireAuth + JWT-derived tenantId from
    req.user. DO NOT replace body.tenant_id with a students.tenant_id
    lookup keyed by body.student_id — that's just trading one body-
    input tenant bind for another. Must land before any external-
    facing release of the snapshot/print feature.

  - [504 v1 SCOPING RESOLUTION — captured at session close,
    executes in Session 30] Forms covered in v1: J + I + C only
    (Form C = Prior Notice and Consent to Evaluate; Form I =
    Section 504 Eligibility Determination; Form J = Section 504
    Student Accommodation Plan). Forms A, B, D, E, F, G, H, K,
    L deferred to Phase 2+. Three architectural decisions locked
    at session close:
      * New student-profile section/tab. Dedicated "504 Plan"
        tab on the student profile, parallel to the existing
        MTSS plan tab. Each student's 504 history lives in this
        section; MTSS plan tab unchanged.
      * Three-layer permission tiers. Parent-visible (e.g.,
        accommodations, team member names + roles, own consent),
        gated (e.g., evaluation consent audit trail, eligibility
        determination notes), and staff-only (admin config such
        as tenant_form_sets). Each table's tier documented at
        schema-design time in PRIVACY_REVIEW.md.
      * Forms J + I + C as v1 deliverable. Phase 2+ adds the
        remaining forms.
    Three additional architectural sketch decisions deferred to
    Session 30 open for owner confirmation: cycle grouping vs
    direct FKs between forms; parent-portal routing as a
    separate file vs filtered shared routes; whether v1 includes
    a minimum parent rights surface. These three were confirmed
    at Session 30 open and executed in PR #20; see Session 30's
    activities.txt entry for details.

  - [504 v1 future request, captured for visibility] Sample upload
    templates. Future feature: allow districts to upload their own
    state-specific 504 form-set templates to seed a tenant_form_sets
    row, paralleling the oregon-ode-2025 form set landed in Session
    30 PR #20. Out of scope for v1 — admins receive ODE-bundled
    form set as the only option. Distinct from sample student-data
    CSV import templates; this is about jurisdiction-specific
    compliance forms, not roster data.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  Followup 81 lesson applied from day one. Every cycle child table has
  both the composite (id, tenant_id) FK reference to its parent AND
  an explicit btree index on the FK column. Cross-tenant child
  references are rejected at the SQL layer regardless of any
  application bug. Prerequisite UNIQUE (id, tenant_id) added to
  existing students table as a pure additive change.
  Followup 67 lesson applied from day one. Every new endpoint in
  routes/student504.js (9 stubs) and routes/parent504.js (2 stubs)
  uses requireAuth + JWT-derived tenantId from req.user. req.body.tenant_id
  is never trusted for tenant scoping. Both privacy-reviewer and
  tenant-isolation-auditor concurrence on this fix shape from
  Session 29 PR #16 review cycle.
  Defense in depth at the route boundary, not just runtime filtering:
  routes/parent504.js physically does not contain handlers for
  staff-only resources (eligibility determinations, evaluation
  consent audit trail). A handler bug attempting to expose staff-
  only data through a parent route is impossible because no such
  handler exists in the file.
  Tenant-isolation-auditor verdict on PR #20: APPROVED WITH NOTES.
    0 CRITICAL, 0 HIGH, 1 MEDIUM, 0 LOW, 4 INFO. The MEDIUM
    (req.targetStudent.tenant_id reference in routes/parent504.js
    Phase-2 SQL comments — middleware actually sets req.student)
    was closed in commit 9 (80cfcf1). The 4 INFO are positive
    verifications: composite (id, tenant_id) FK pattern across
    all 504 child tables; tenant_id NOT NULL with supporting
    btree indexes from day one; students UNIQUE (id, tenant_id)
    constraint addition; JSONB tenant scoping inheritance on
    student_504_plans.accommodations.

ITERATION HISTORY (process notes for §4B-scrubbed audit trail)
  Pattern B → Pattern A middleware composition for refuseParentRole.
    Original audit plan staged route scaffolds with Pattern B
    (function-call inside handler body: if (refuseParentRole(req, res))
    return;). Scoping discussion before commit 2 was pushed surfaced
    three reasons to switch to Pattern A (middleware in route
    definition: [requireAuth, refuseParentRole, async handler]):
    project-precedent consistency with every other auth-gated route
    in the codebase from Sessions 25/27/28; grep-ability so a single
    `grep "refuseParentRole" routes/student504.js` returns the route
    definitions themselves making the gate auditable from the route
    table at a glance; forgetting risk in PR 2's 9 real handlers
    where a missing if-return at the top of a new handler under
    Pattern B would be silently fatal. Pattern A was the right call
    from the start. Splitting commits to fake a B → A sequence that
    didn't happen on disk would have been historical fiction; the
    "we considered B" trail lives in the session conversation
    transcript and in this entry. Captured as a memory for future
    sessions.
  Source-document format issue. First-pass download of Forms C, I, J
    landed as macOS Pages source documents (.pages binary format)
    rather than PDFs. Re-exported via macOS Pages → PDF for commit 4.
    Internal PDF title metadata still carries trailing whitespace
    from that export flow but the filenames are clean and follow
    the README's convention. No content fidelity loss.
  Form J PDF file content mismatch (commit 4 amend recovery).
    The file saved as
    docs/references/oregon-ode-2025-form-j-accommodation-plan.pdf
    actually contained Form I (Eligibility Determination) content.
    Both PDFs passed all of CC's mechanical checks (file, pdfinfo,
    byte-size). Cowork independently read the uploaded files via
    its Read tool and caught the duplicate. Owner re-exported
    Form J from Pages with the correct source. Commit 4 was already
    pushed (sha b4f47b6) when the mismatch was caught; resolved via
    git commit --amend --no-edit + git push --force-with-lease on
    the personal feature branch (post-amend sha 7dd7a7d). Force-
    with-lease vs bare force is the safety belt; pattern only
    acceptable pre-PR-open on personal branches (no PR was open at
    amend time).
  Form set content checkpoint (commit 5 pre-write discipline).
    Cowork did a content-correctness checkpoint on CC's pre-write
    form set proposal, verifying all quoted material (23 major
    life activities, 3 accommodation domains, signature-line
    wording, eligibility outcome options, parent consent text)
    against the actual source PDFs. Three structural fixes pre-
    write: knowledgeableOf type changed from 'multiCheckbox' to
    'radio' (form says "check one"); meetingParticipants columns
    expanded from 2 collapsed columns to 4 distinct (Name |
    Signature | Agree | Disagree, with Agree/Disagree as
    independent checkboxes preserving the "both blank" form
    state); planDates.heading corrected from a misread first half
    of a wrapping field label to the neutral "Plan Dates" heading.
    No file already on disk; checkpoint happened during pre-write
    proposal review.
  Migration 022 schema reshape after reading actual ODE Form J.
    Migration 021's first-pass shape modeled accommodations as rows
    in a separate student_504_accommodations child table with a
    category enum. After reading the actual ODE Form J end-to-end
    for commit 5's form set definition, the accommodations on the
    form turned out to be naturally per-domain free-form blocks
    (educational / extracurricular / assessments), not enum-typed
    rows. Migration 022 dropped student_504_accommodations and
    added an accommodations JSONB column to student_504_plans,
    keyed by the form set's domain keys. Same-PR iteration was
    safe because the table held no rows (PR 1 routes are stubs);
    a future cherry-pick split would invalidate that safety, hence
    the deploy-window guarantee captured above.

  --- Closed by this entry ---
  - PR #20 (504 v1 foundation) — merged 2026-04-28T00:39:13Z, merge
    commit cb3c603, deployed and smoke-verified.
  - §2A discussion (paused at end of Session 31) — resolved via

  --- New (Session 32) ---
  - [new; Session 32 deploy verification] /version endpoint. Add a
    /version route on the backend returning
    { commit: process.env.RENDER_GIT_COMMIT }. ~5 line change in
    server.js. Reason: today's deploy verification fell back to a
    404→401 transition signal because no last-modified / age /
    x-render-build-id headers are exposed on this Render deployment;
    that transition closed in <80s before the baseline probe could
    capture the 404 state. A /version endpoint that returns the
    deployed commit SHA gives an unambiguous, latency-independent
    deploy-confirmation signal next time.
  - [new; Session 32 documentation] Source comment on
    GET /api/parent-504/procedural-safeguards documenting why
    requireAuth alone (no per-student or role guard) is intentional.
    Per the file's design notes the safeguards text is per-tenant
    identical, no studentId is in the path, and no per-student
    access check applies. It is currently the only requireAuth-only
    handler in the new 504 surface and reads as a possible missing
    guard during cold review without context. Single-comment fix.
  - [new; Session 32 operations] Document Render auto-deploy latency
    baseline for tiertrak-api.onrender.com. Observed ~38s from merge
    push to dashboard "Live" status today, including Migrations 021
    + 022. Worth recording in docs/ai-context/STACK_ARCHITECTURE.md
    or a new operational runbook so future merge plans do not budget
    multi-minute verification windows that close in under a minute.
  - [new; Session 32 process] Polling-loop pattern reconsideration.
    The 30s/10min poll loop assumes a multi-minute deploy window;
    today's evidence is that for small Express apps on Render with
    quick migrations, the window can close before the first probe
    fires. Future verification should either (a) capture the
    pre-merge baseline before triggering the merge command (not
    after), or (b) skip polling entirely and rely on dashboard
    "Live" event + a one-shot post-Live probe.

  --- New (Session 31) — carry forward as future-followup candidates ---
  - GitHub auto-delete-on-merge toggle verification. Session 31
    surfaced intermittent behavior (fired for PR #21, not PR #22);
    Session 32 confirms it also did not fire for PR #20. Worth a
    30-second check of repo Settings → General → "Automatically
    delete head branches" toggle when next in GitHub UI.
  - Owner-developer framing reconciliation. Project's custom
    instructions frame owner as non-technical; owner asserted in
    Session 31 they are the developer of this project. §2A path-1
    resolution in Session 32 means this framing is no longer blocking
    — separation of duties is satisfied via the independent reviewer
    regardless of owner's role. Decision deferred; not urgent.

  --- New (Session 34) ---
  - [new; Session 34 reviewer feedback] Rate limiting on POST
    endpoints. Repo-wide grep finds zero express-rate-limit /
    rateLimit usage. Security-reviewer LOW-2 — not introduced by
    this branch (consistent with existing precedent), tracked as
    a separate backlog item. Recommend a sec/ branch adding
    express-rate-limit globally to /api/student-504 and
    /api/parent-504 POSTs, then expanding to legacy POST surface.
  - [new; Session 34 reviewer feedback] accommodations JSONB
    depth/size guard. Security-reviewer LOW-3 — POST /plans
    accepts a plain JSON object via isPlainJsonObject but does not
    cap byte size or nesting depth. A staff caller already
    authenticated and tenant-bound could push a multi-MB JSONB
    that the parent then reads back. Recommend a server-side
    JSON.stringify(accommodations).length < N_KB guard. Small
    follow-up; not a regression.
  - [new; Session 34 reviewer feedback] requireStudentReadAccess
    middleware logs err.message. Tenant-isolation-auditor LOW L1 —
    the middleware (pre-existing in
    middleware/authorizeInterventionAccess.js:229) still uses
    console.error('[requireStudentReadAccess]', err.message)
    which can carry PG row values that count as PII. Out of scope
    for PR #24 (no diff line touches the middleware), but worth a
    fix-it pass on all middleware err.message logs in a sec/ or
    chore/ branch.
  - [new; Session 34 deployment] Pin Node version explicitly. No
    render.yaml, no .nvmrc, no engines field in package.json;
    production Node version is set only in the Render dashboard
    (verified 22.22.0 today). PR #24's FORM_SET_REGISTRY pattern
    needs ≥22.12 to work; a future dashboard change to <22.12
    would silently break server start. Add engines: { "node":
    ">=22.12" } to package.json and either a .nvmrc for local-dev
    parity or a minimal render.yaml pinning the build env.
    Captured in PR #24 description.
  - [new; Session 34 architecture] Form-set architecture pass.
    PR #24's FORM_SET_REGISTRY require()s a frontend ESM module
    from the backend (frontend/src/data/504-form-sets/...);
    functional but cross-tree. Long-term: move form-set content
    to a shared/ directory imported by both, OR store form-set
    content in the database keyed by form_set_id. TODO(form-set-
    arch) breadcrumb left in the code comment. Not refactored
    in PR 2.
  - [new; Session 34 hygiene] Free-text length-cap pass. Repo-wide
    grep found one cap pattern (routes/tier1-assessments.js:355,
    300 chars on tier1 notes); every other free-text field is
    uncapped. Adopt a generous-but-finite cap (e.g., 10000 chars)
    on all uncapped TEXT-typed fields across routes/ as
    defense-in-depth. Surfaced during PR #24 commit 2 length-cap
    decision search.
  - [new; Session 34 tooling] IDE-extension vs terminal claude
    subagent-registration asymmetry. The VS Code Claude extension
    session in which this branch was developed does NOT register
    project-defined subagents from .claude/agents/*.md; the Agent
    tool only exposed claude-code-guide / Explore / general-
    purpose / Plan / statusline-setup. The fresh terminal claude
    session that owner ran for the three reviewer subagents DID
    register them and produced clean approves. Worth documenting
    in docs/ai-context/ as a process note: project subagents must
    be invoked from a terminal claude session, not from the IDE
    extension, until/unless the IDE extension catches up. Possible
    upstream issue to file with Anthropic.

  --- Held; carried from Session 32 ---
  - /version endpoint (Session 32 followup #1). Add /version
    returning { commit: process.env.RENDER_GIT_COMMIT } in
    server.js. ~5 line change. Reason: PR #24 deploy will need
    verification; without /version, the only signals are the
    404→401 transition (Session 32 lesson: closes too fast on
    small Express + Render — under 80s) and the dashboard "Live"
    status. /version is the durable fix.
  - Render auto-deploy latency baseline doc (Session 32 followup
    #3). Observed ~38s merge-to-Live in Session 32. Recordable
    in docs/ai-context/STACK_ARCHITECTURE.md.

    #4). 30s/10min poll loop assumes multi-minute deploy windows;
    Render evidence is sub-minute. Future: capture pre-merge
    baseline BEFORE triggering merge, or skip polling.
  - CLI-based authenticated smoke check (Session 32 followup #5).
    Browser cross-origin cookie limitations meant the
    authenticated regression couldn't be completed via address-
    bar navigation. Document a curl-from-local-env-var workflow.

    Oregon ODE 2025 form set (form_set_id='oregon-ode-2025',
    form_set_version='08/2023', is_active=t). Lookup by exact tenants.
    name with count-check preflight (mirrors the seed-humble-isd-
    sandbox.js pattern; tenants.name is not UNIQUE in schema.sql so a
    defensive count check raises on miss-or-ambiguous). ON CONFLICT
    (tenant_id, form_set_id) DO NOTHING for idempotent rerun. Post-
    commit SELECT confirmed 2 rows, both is_active=t.

  - student_504_cycles: 1 row deleted.
    tenant_id 4, cycle id 2; an accidental empty cycle. Preflight
    verified zero rows in all four child tables (student_504_evaluation_
    consents, student_504_eligibility_determinations, student_504_plans,
    student_504_team_members). DELETE was tenant-scoped (id AND
    tenant_id in WHERE clause). Composite-FK ON DELETE CASCADE on the
    four child tables would have cascaded if non-empty, but the
    preflight made that unreachable. student_504_accommodations is
    transitive via plans (FK to plans, not cycles); plans=0 implies
    accommodations=0. Post-commit SELECT confirmed remaining=0.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  No new SQL queries on the FE branch (PR #31). The three production
  DB writes (2 INSERT + 1 DELETE) were all tenant-scoped via Render
  psql DO blocks. Every WHERE clause carried both id and tenant_id;

  rather than a client-supplied value. No cross-tenant reads or writes

  PR #31) verified the backend pattern is intact at HEAD for all four
  504 staff resources and confirmed migration 021's composite-FK + UNIQUE
  (id, tenant_id) constraints are intact across the six tenant-scoped
  504 tables plus tenant_form_sets.

  - One Oregon production tenant (tenant_id=8) has no subdomain set in the

    resolution code path keys off subdomain, this tenant may currently
    lack a working portal URL. Worth investigating whether provisioning
    gap (tenant onboarded before subdomain was wired into auth?) or
    intentional configuration. Not blocking the form-set registration
    that landed this session.

PR #33 — fix/csv-import-icon-import → main (MERGED)
  Title: fix(students): add missing FileSpreadsheet icon import
  URL:   https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/33
  Hot fix for a production crash on the staff Import CSV surface. In
  production the bundle index-Cumojc4B.js threw "Uncaught
  ReferenceError: FileSpreadsheet is not defined" the moment a staff
  user clicked Import CSV — the modal's header rendered
  <FileSpreadsheet /> at App.jsx:5662 and the modal's button rendered
  it again at App.jsx:5713, but the symbol was never added to the
  lucide-react import block at App.jsx:4. Fix was a one-line additive
  insertion of FileSpreadsheet next to FileText. Diff: 1+/1- (trailing
  whitespace deliberately preserved so the diff was purely additive).
  Pre-fix verified the symbol is a real lucide-react export; post-fix
  vite build clean (only the carried-forward 500 kB chunk advisory)
  and zero new lint errors on the touched line. No reviewer subagents
  required (icon-import-only render fix touches no PII / auth / SQL /
  tenant surfaces); §2A self-merge per CLAUDE.md.

  Smoke-test design pivot: the standard local-DB smoke test path
  (start backend locally, override VITE_API_URL, reset the local
  seed test admin password via scripts/seed-tier1-local-test.js, log
  in) was abandoned
  mid-session after the wasted-effort discovery that the localhost
  Vite frontend reads VITE_API_URL from .env which points at the
  production Render backend. The local-DB password reset therefore
  had no effect on the localhost frontend's auth flow. Pivoted to:
  localhost frontend (with the fix) authenticated against the
  production Render backend with real production credentials, modal-
  renders verification only, no CSV uploaded. Render-only fix →
  modal-renders is the full surface area. After merge + Render auto-
  deploy, repeated the verification on the live production site with
  hard-refresh + DevTools open — modal opened clean, icon visible,
  zero FileSpreadsheet errors. No data written at any point in the
  smoke-test loop.

  Engineering note worth banking: in this codebase's current dev
  setup, `npm --prefix frontend run dev` does NOT auto-wire to a
  local backend — it reads VITE_API_URL from .env, which currently
  resolves to production. Verifying VITE_API_URL is a required
  preflight before any local-DB-side smoke-test prep. SMOKE_TESTING.md
  (Followup #11) needs this in its first paragraph.

PR #30 — chore/activities-log-session-38 → main (AUTO-MERGED via reachability)
  URL: https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/30
  Closed-as-merged automatically when PR #32 landed. Mechanism:
  GitHub auto-merges a PR transitively when its head commit becomes
  reachable from the base by any path. PR #30's head was ed44d9b
  (the Session 38 entry, the parent of PR #32's branch), so the
  moment 9cf0b01 landed on main, ed44d9b was reachable and GitHub
  flipped PR #30 to MERGED at 2026-04-30T17:24:01Z (one second after
  PR #32) with no separate merge commit produced. Audit-trail comment
  posted explaining the mechanism for future reviewers.

  Engineering note worth banking: when stacking a chore PR off
  another chore PR (which we did here for the Session 38b
  continuation), expect transitive auto-merge rather than manual
  closure. `gh pr close` will reject the close attempt with "can't
  be closed because it was already merged". If audit-trail comments
  matter, post them via `gh pr comment` (which works on merged PRs)
  rather than chaining them to `gh pr close`.

  (since they don't use 504), and possibly a slimmer student roster
  appropriate for the demo size. Decide whether Followup #26 (hide
  504 tab) is a fast-follow that lands first so the Honduras tenant
  has a clean product surface from day one.

  probe in incognito) — still parked from PR #31 pre-merge, ~5

  runtime route-level evidence at the route layer.

PR #35 — feat/504-tab-hide-on-no-formset → main (MERGED)
  Title: feat(504): hide Section 504 tab on tenants with no active form_set
  URL:   https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/35

  Followup #26. Adds a tenant-scoped GET /api/student-504/active-
  form-set endpoint that returns the calling tenant's currently
  active form set (form_set_id + form_set_version) or 404 when no
  active row exists. The Section 504 staff tab now fetches this on
  mount and returns null from the component when no active form set
  is configured — covering both the in-flight initial state and the
  genuinely-unconfigured state with a single overloaded null check.

  For tenants like Lincoln/Parkview seed and the new prospect
  provisioned this session, staff no longer see a 504 tab that
  crashes with a 400 error envelope when "Start 504 Cycle" is
  clicked. The backend gate at POST /cycles continues to enforce
  tenant_form_sets-presence authoritatively; this PR added only the
  FE-side visibility match.

      parent504.js-precedent 404 shape, ORDER BY created_at DESC
      LIMIT 1 tiebreaker for the multi-active-row schema edge case.

      getActiveFormSet wrapper routed through send() (existing
      house style; throws on non-OK).

      + parallel fetch in existing useEffect, single-null-check
      render gate, runtime form-set values replacing compile-time
      oregon-ode-2025 import.

PR #36 — chore/seed-tenant-sandbox-template → main (MERGED with security-reviewer notes)
  Title: chore(seed): add generic sandbox tenant seeder + schema template
  URL:   https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/36

  Adds scripts/seed-tenant-sandbox-template.js (697 lines) — a
  school-agnostic generic version of the existing Humble seed
  script. Takes a roster file at an absolute path via --roster,
  validates the roster against a strict schema, generates per-user
  bcrypt-hashed credentials, and emits a single BEGIN/COMMIT-
  wrapped DO block to stdout for hand-pasting into Render psql.
  The script does not connect to a database directly.

  Also adds data/sandbox-roster-template.js (158 lines) — a
  committed placeholder-only schema-doc file with a §4B privacy
  banner instructing operators to copy outside the repo, populate
  with synthetic data, and pass the absolute path to the seed
  script. Path B convention per the Session 39 master index §4B
  question is now established: school-specific roster data lives
  in workspace, never in the repo.

  Pre-implementation audit + Owner review approved three design
  decisions:
    - Index-based PL/pgSQL variable scheme (v_user_1..v_user_N)
      derived from [...ADMINS, ...STAFF] order, with trailing
      SQL comments naming each user for operator readability.
    - --dry-run flag removed (the script has no other mode; a
      no-op alias was misleading tech debt).
    - Dollar-quote tag sanitized (subdomain → [a-zA-Z0-9_] with
      'sandbox' fallback) to prevent identifier-collision on
      hyphen-bearing subdomains.

  Plus three Owner-approved refinements during implementation:
    - Validator error messages naming offending key paths and
      valid-option enums (priority on the new
      school_wide_access-required check, since Humble-pattern
      operators migrating to the generic script will hit it).
    - Sanitized dollar-quote tag verified against test roster.
    - Empty-array guards on STAFF / INTERVENTIONS / PROGRESS_NOTES
      / PARENT_LINK confirmed by visual inspection.

  Existing scripts/seed-humble-isd-sandbox.js and data/humble-isd-
  sandbox-roster.js intentionally untouched in this branch.
  Retiring the legacy pair is a separate decision for after the
  generic script has been exercised on a real new tenant.

  Reviewer subagents at PR time:
    - tenant-isolation-auditor: APPROVED, 0 CRITICAL / 0 WARN /
      5 INFO (10 emitted DML statements + 3 preflight SELECTs all
      properly tenant-scoped via v_tenant_id captured from
      RETURNING id; parent_student_links's known no-tenant_id gap
      explicitly cited).
    - security-reviewer: APPROVED WITH NOTES, 0 CRITICAL / 0
      HIGH / 1 MEDIUM / 1 LOW / 8 INFO. The MEDIUM (defense-in-
      depth: TENANT.name interpolated raw into two SQL line-
      comment headers; rejecting \r/\n in the validator would
      close the only non-trivial SQL-injection surface) was
      Owner-acknowledged and tracked as Followup #29; PR landed
      as-is per Owner decision.
    - privacy-reviewer: APPROVED, 0 CRITICAL / 0 HIGH / 0
      MEDIUM / 2 LOW / 9 INFO. Both LOWs (validator JSON.stringify
      truncation, unknown-key warning) tracked as Followups
      #30 + #31, non-blocking.

  §2A non-Owner human review: APPROVED by Owner's ed tech
  specialist.

  Verification: node -c on both files clean; CLI gauntlet (4 tests:
  no-args / relative path / non-.js extension / absolute path to
  existing humble-isd-sandbox-roster.js) all passing with expected
  exit codes; sanitized dollar-quote tag verified
  (humble-isd-demo → humble_isd_demo).

PRODUCTION DB WRITES (Render psql one-shots, BEGIN/COMMIT, DO blocks)

  Tenant provisioning for the prospect (tenant_id=10):
    - tenants: 1 row created. Type='school', subdomain set to
      a sanitized prospect-specific slug.
    - users: 8 rows created. 1 school admin (real prospect
      contact email) + 5 synthetic teachers + 1 synthetic
      counselor + 1 synthetic demo parent. Synthetic accounts
      use the IETF-reserved .example TLD on a sandbox-specific
      subdomain so emails cannot accidentally route to a real
      mail system.
    - students: 270 rows. K-12, ~21 per grade. Tier
      distribution: 200 / 55 / 15 (T1 / T2 / T3). Synthetic
      Latin American + American name mix appropriate for an
      international school in the prospect's country.
    - student_interventions: 30 rows initially (15 T3 + 15 T2
      students each receiving one intervention). Templates
      drawn from 14 distinct system-default
      intervention_templates (verified against the production
      bank via a read-only psql query during a preflight
      iteration; first attempt failed Preflight 3 due to
      template name mismatches — see "Catches" below).
    - progress_notes: 61 rows initially. 1-3 notes per
      intervention'd student, dated 1-91 days ago.
    - parent_student_links: 1 row.
    - tenant_intervention_bank: 61 rows auto-seeded mirroring
      routes/tenants.js's auto-seed for is_starter=TRUE
      templates.

    Single atomic DO block, three preflight RAISE EXCEPTIONs
    (subdomain uniqueness, email uniqueness, intervention-
    template name match-count = 1), and a final RAISE NOTICE
    confirming the tenant id post-commit.

  Cleanup operation immediately after provisioning:
    - student_interventions: 2 rows deleted.
    - progress_notes: 5 rows deleted.

    These were unexpected duplicates surfaced when the post-commit
    verification SELECT showed counts of 32/66 instead of the
    expected 30/61. Diagnosed as the seed script's emit-time
    name-based reference resolution: the roster contained two
    duplicate first+last name combinations across grade levels
    (Student-A grade 1 / grade 6, Student-B grade 1 / grade 5),
    and the script's INSERT INTO student_interventions
    statements use a (first_name, last_name) WHERE clause that
    matched both colliding student rows for each duplicate name.
    Tier 1 students inadvertently received the same interventions
    as their colliding T2/T3 namesakes. Surgical cleanup via a
    tenant-scoped DELETE on (student.tenant_id = 10 AND
    student.tier = 1) for both tables, verified counts dropped
    to 30 and 61 inside a transaction before COMMIT. Tracked
    long-form as Followup #33.

  Password rotation later in session (recovery from chat-rendering
  mismatch — see Followup #34 / "Catches" below):
    - users: 8 rows updated. password_hash + updated_at refreshed
      via UPDATE statements wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT. Plaintexts
      generated locally in the operator's terminal (not via CC's
      bash tool) so they bypass any chat-rendering concerns; SQL
      file contained only bcrypt hashes for hand-paste into psql.
      All 8 logins verified post-rotation.

CATCHES (Cowork-side and Claude Code-side observations worth banking)

  1. Provisioning preflight discipline. The seed script's three
     RAISE EXCEPTION preflights (subdomain unique, email unique,
     intervention-template name match) caught the first
     provisioning attempt's template-name mismatch cleanly: the
     entire transaction rolled back with zero partial state. No
     manual cleanup needed; just regenerate the roster with valid
     template names and retry. This is the script's atomic-
     transaction design working as intended.

  2. Roster template names must match production intervention_
     templates exactly. First attempt used 4 plausible-sounding
     names (Reading Intervention, Math Intervention, Behavior
     Support Plan, Check-In/Check-Out (CICO)) that don't exist
     in the production system-default bank. Production has 70
     templates with very specific names (e.g., the actual entry
     is "Check-in/Check-out" with lowercase "in" and no
     parenthetical). For future tenant provisionings, query
     intervention_templates WHERE tenant_id IS NULL ORDER BY name
     before drafting roster INTERVENTIONS to avoid this loop.

  3. Schema knowledge gaps from Cowork-side cost iteration. Two
     verification queries failed mid-session due to wrong column
     name assumptions: student_interventions has no tenant_id
     column (scoping is via student_id → students.tenant_id);
     student_interventions's template FK column is
     intervention_template_id, not template_id. Both surfaced via
     ERROR responses and were quickly corrected. Worth banking:
     when writing verification queries against tenant-scoped
     tables, prefer JOINs through the canonical tenant-scoping
     column rather than assuming a redundant tenant_id on every
     table.

  4. Seed script reference resolution by name is fragile. The
     seed script's INSERT INTO student_interventions and
     PROGRESS_NOTES statements resolve student references by
     (first_name, last_name) WHERE clauses rather than by
     external_id (or by tracking the INSERT...RETURNING student
     ids in a JS-side map). Synthetic rosters at any reasonable
     scale will have name collisions (the 270-student roster
     produced 2 duplicate name pairs by chance via the random
     name generator). When a name collides, the script inserts
     the same intervention/note against EVERY matching student
     — which surfaced as +2 interventions and +5 progress notes
     attached to Tier 1 students that should have had none.
     Cleanup was straightforward (tenant-scoped DELETE on T1
     students for those two tables) and the underlying tenant
     scoping was correct. But the script should be hardened:
     resolve references by external_id (tracked via INSERT...
     RETURNING) rather than by name lookup. Tracked as
     Followup #33.

  5. CC chat rendering and password fidelity. The original
     prospect provisioning's plaintext passwords (output by the
     seed script via CC's bash tool, captured as part of CC's
     tool result, then rendered into CC chat) did not bcrypt-
     verify against the corresponding hashes in the database.
     CC's standalone test on a tiny isolated roster showed all
     plaintext + hash pairs verifying correctly — the script's
     emit logic is provably sound. So the corruption happened
     between the script's stderr and the operator's clipboard
     (the chat-rendering layer). Most likely candidates: markdown
     processing in the chat UI transformed a base64url character
     (- ↔ em-dash, smart-quote substitution); column-alignment-
     induced copy-paste error; or some other subtle character-
     substitution in the rendered table. Recovery worked
     around the issue cleanly: a rotation script was run in the
     operator's local terminal (NOT via CC's bash tool), so
     plaintexts went directly to terminal scrollback (byte-
     accurate); UPDATE statements via psql replaced the original
     hashes; logins verified post-rotation. Future password-
     handling work follows the same rule: seed scripts run in
     the operator's terminal, never via CC's bash tool. Tracked
     as Followup #34.

  6. Cowork-side miss on the password-handling channel. The
     initial seed run for the new prospect was via CC's bash
     tool, which placed plaintexts in the chat transcript. CC
     flagged this correctly at the time ("Plaintext passwords
     are now in this conversation transcript. That's an inherent
     side effect of running the script via the Bash tool").
     Cowork-side did not redirect the seed script run to the
     operator's local terminal at that point — that decision
     would have prevented both the rotation requirement AND the
     chat-rendering mismatch issue downstream. For Session 41+:
     the SMOKE_TESTING.md walkthrough (Followup #11) and any
     future password-related operational doc must lead with
     "run in your local terminal, never via CC's bash tool."

  7. Atomic transaction recovery works as designed. Three
     transactions were attempted today (initial seed, cleanup,
     password rotation) and one of them rolled back cleanly on a
     preflight RAISE EXCEPTION. Zero partial-state cleanup
     needed across the day. The DO-block + BEGIN/COMMIT envelope
     plus RAISE EXCEPTION preflights remain the load-bearing
     pattern for any production DB write involving multiple
     tables.

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS

  --- Net new from Session 40 ---
  - #27 NEW — Reconcile users.role CHECK constraint between repo
    migrations and production. Production CHECK accepts 8 roles
    (district_admin, school_admin, teacher, counselor,
    behavior_specialist, student_support_specialist, mtss_support,
    parent); repo migration-001-update-roles.sql defines only 5.
    Drift suggests prod was hand-widened or a migration was
    drafted but never committed. Reconcile before any
    fresh-environment deploy (e.g., a staging environment) where
    the repo migrations would be replayed and produce a CHECK
    constraint missing 3 roles.
  - #28 NEW — tenant_form_sets schema: add a partial unique index
    `(tenant_id) WHERE is_active = TRUE` to enforce
    one-active-row-per-tenant at the schema level. Currently
    handled at the runtime tiebreaker (ORDER BY created_at DESC
    LIMIT 1) in both routes/parent504.js and the new
    /active-form-set endpoint, but it's a smell that the schema
    permits multiple active rows. Tracked as out-of-scope for
    PR #35 by the tenant-isolation-auditor.
  - #29 NEW (security-reviewer M1) — Generic seed script: add
    \r/\n rejection in validateRoster for TENANT.name,
    TENANT.type, ADMINS[i].full_name, STAFF[i].full_name. Closes
    the only non-trivial SQL-injection surface (TENANT.name is
    interpolated raw into two -- SQL line-comment headers; a
    roster with embedded newlines could break out of the comment
    and inject SQL ahead of BEGIN). Reviewer rated MEDIUM
    defense-in-depth, Owner accepted as non-blocking; this
    followup closes it.
  - #30 NEW (privacy-reviewer LOW-1) — Generic seed script:
    truncate JSON.stringify output in validator error messages
    to a fixed length (~80 chars) so accidentally-pasted real PII
    in a roster cannot splash a full identifying record across
    stderr. Defensive against operator-side mistakes; not a §4B
    violation per the reviewer (operator-terminal stderr ≠
    frontend/log/tracking) but worth closing.
  - #31 NEW (privacy-reviewer LOW-2) — Generic seed script:
    either warn on unknown roster keys, OR enumerate the
    accepted optional fields in the committed template. Hygiene
    suggestion to prevent operators from typo'ing a field name
    (e.g., 'gradle' instead of 'grade') and not realizing the
    field was silently discarded.
  - #32 NEW — Generic seed script: allow ADMINS roster entries
    to specify their own role explicitly instead of hardcoding
    'district_admin'. For schools that aren't districts (e.g.,
    international or charter schools), 'school_admin' is the
    natural role; hardcoding district_admin is a Humble-pattern
    artifact that doesn't fit the general case. Functionally
    harmless (district_admin >= school_admin in permissions) but
    cosmetically wrong in any UI surface that displays role.
  - #33 NEW — Generic seed script: resolve roster references by
    external_id rather than by (first_name, last_name) name
    lookup. Real-world synthetic rosters at any reasonable scale
    will have name collisions; the current behavior inserts
    intervention/note rows against EVERY matching student,
    duplicating data across colliding namesakes. Cleanest fix:
    track student_external_id → DB students.id in a JS-side map
    via INSERT...RETURNING, then reference that map for all
    downstream INSERTs (interventions, notes, parent links).
    Same pattern as varByEmail for users.
  - #34 NEW — Investigate the CC chat rendering / password
    fidelity issue. The seed script's plaintext output, when
    captured via CC's bash tool and rendered into chat,
    produced password values that did not bcrypt-verify against
    the corresponding hashes that landed in the database. The
    script itself is provably correct (CC's isolated-roster
    repro showed all plaintext+hash pairs verifying). The
    corruption happened between stderr and clipboard.
    Diagnostic should: (a) write a small repro that runs the
    seed script in CC's bash tool and dumps both rendered chat
    output and raw stderr bytes to a file, (b) byte-by-byte
    diff to identify which character(s) were transformed,
    (c) document the affected character set in a Cowork-facing
    operational note. Workaround already established (run the
    script in the operator's local terminal, never via CC's
    bash tool); this followup is for understanding, not for
    unblocking customer work.

  --- Followups carried forward unchanged from Session 39 ---
  - #11 docs(dev): SMOKE_TESTING.md walkthrough.
    PRIORITY UPGRADED AGAIN. Today's session cost ~30 min on
    chat-rendering / password handling friction in addition to
    Session 39's prior friction. SMOKE_TESTING.md should
    consolidate: (a) verify VITE_API_URL before any local-DB
    work; (b) seed scripts run in the operator's local
    terminal, never via CC's bash tool; (c) password rotation
    pattern via UPDATE SQL when chat exposure happens; (d) the
    Render psql one-shot pattern for production data ops.
  - #22 .claude/settings.json drift cleanup. Diagnostic still
    holds: file auto-regenerates on every branch switch.
  - All other followups from Session 39's consolidated list
    carry forward unchanged.

  --- Stale local + remote refs ---
  - Carryover from Session 39: chore/activities-log-session-36,
    chore/activities-log-session-38, chore/seed-humble-isd-
    sandbox, feat/504-form-cij-frontend (local); origin/chore/
    activities-log, origin/fix/resource-1-4-calendar-
    underscores, origin/fix/csv-import-icon-import (remote
    only).
  - Plus today's: feat/504-tab-hide-on-no-formset and chore/
    seed-tenant-sandbox-template should be cleanly deletable
    locally and remotely after the merges (--delete-branch was
    used, but verify next quiet moment).

PR #38 — docs/followup-34-chat-rendering → main (MERGED)
  Title: docs(operational-notes): CC chat rendering and password
         fidelity findings (Followup #34)
  URL:   https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/38
  Merged 2026-05-01T16:10:38Z, merge commit 503dd93.

  Closes Followup #34. New file
  docs/operational-notes/cc-chat-rendering.md (129 lines)
  documenting a three-point byte-diff diagnostic of CC's chat-
  rendering pipeline. Repro script lives in operator workspace
  (Path B, never committed); only the findings note enters the
  repo.

  Diagnostic methodology:
    - Point A: operator's local terminal direct stderr, byte-faithful
      ground truth (`node <script> 2> /tmp/A.bin`).
    - Point B: CC bash tool stderr-to-disk via redirect, then xxd to
      disk (`node <script> 2> /tmp/B.bin && xxd /tmp/B.bin > /tmp/B.hex`).
    - Point C: CC chat panel rendered output, copied by operator
      into local file, then xxd locally.
    - Pairwise diffs: A-vs-B isolates bash-tool capture; B-vs-C
      isolates chat-rendering plus clipboard; A-vs-C is end-to-end.

  Three findings:
    1. CR (0x0D) → LF (0x0A) in-place byte substitution at the
       chat-display-to-clipboard layer. Single-byte transform,
       identical file size, observed at byte offset 0x44b in
       TEST 25 carriageReturn. Reproducible. Severity: moderate.
       The only character-level transform observed across the
       entire 33-test battery.
    2. Line-level structural corruption in CC's bash-tool-result-
       to-chat-display layer. Observed once when CC reported
       `head -40 /tmp/B.hex` to chat: line 0x60's right-side
       ASCII gloss column showed line 0x10's gloss content while
       the hex column remained correct. The on-disk /tmp/B.hex
       was correct (the operator verified locally). One
       observation, NOT deliberately reproduced — characterized
       in the doc as "worth banking, not isolated bug." Severity:
       unknown. The most plausible candidate explanation for
       Session 40's bcrypt-verify failures, but suggestive rather
       than confirmed.
    3. Lazy-render selection truncation in VS Code's CC chat
       panel. Operator's first attempt to select-and-copy the
       full chat output truncated at TEST 18 of 33 because
       off-screen content wasn't in the DOM. Silent data loss,
       no error indicator. Severity: high. Workaround: scroll
       the chat panel to the bottom of the rendered content
       before select-all.

  Notable non-findings (Session 40's hypothesis NOT supported):
    - Base64url alphabet (TESTs 01-04, including hyphen runs and
      underscore runs) survived clean.
    - Smart-typography candidates (TESTs 05-10): no em-dash
      transforms, no curly quotes, no ellipsis collapse, no
      apostrophe smart-quoting.
    - Markdown attractor characters (TESTs 11-16): all preserved.
    - Backslash escapes, multi-space runs, HTML angle brackets,
      control bytes (tab, NUL, ESC) — all preserved.
    - Column-aligned table (TEST 31): full multi-row table came
      through byte-clean.
    - Adversarial cell-edge tests (TESTs 32, 33) targeting the
      "trailing hyphen at base64url cell edge becomes em-dash"
      hypothesis specifically: did NOT corrupt.

  Operational rule (run in operator's local terminal, never via
  CC's bash tool, for sensitive output) reinforced, not loosened.

  No reviewer subagents required (doc-only, no PII, no SQL, no
  routes; permission-by-omission framing). §2A self-merge.

PR #39 — docs/followup-11-smoke-testing → main (MERGED)
  Title: docs(operational-notes): smoke-testing runbook
         (Followup #11)
  URL:   https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/39

  Closes Followup #11. New file
  docs/operational-notes/smoke-testing.md (176 lines) consolidating
  operational rules from Sessions 38b/39/40 into a runbook for
  future operators.

  Six body sections:
    1. VITE_API_URL preflight before any local-DB work.
    2. Production data ops via Render psql (one-shot pattern with
       BEGIN/COMMIT + RAISE EXCEPTION preflights, RAISE NOTICE
       post-commit, /tmp/<file>.sql cleanup).
    3. Seed scripts and password handling (the local-terminal
       rule; cites cc-chat-rendering.md for the WHY, this doc is
       the HOW). Section explicitly says do NOT redirect stderr
       to a file (`2> passwords.txt` and equivalents); plaintexts
       must stay in volatile terminal scrollback. Includes Cmd-K
       scrollback clearing as post-delivery hygiene.
    4. Roster template-name verification (the
       `SELECT name FROM intervention_templates WHERE tenant_id
       IS NULL ORDER BY name` pre-flight before drafting any
       roster's INTERVENTIONS array).
    5. Password rotation when chat exposure happens (UPDATE
       skeleton in BEGIN/COMMIT envelope; hashes only in SQL;
       plaintexts hand-delivered out-of-band).
    6. VS Code chat panel lazy-render selection-truncation gotcha
       (cites cc-chat-rendering.md Finding 3).

  Plus a scannable pre-flight checklist (8 items mapping to the
  body sections) and a Related section. Master-index aspirational
  name SMOKE_TESTING.md resolves to this kebab-case path via a
  one-line note at the top of the doc.

  Establishes "note" vs "runbook" genre split inside
  docs/operational-notes/: cc-chat-rendering.md is a "note"
  (postmortem / findings); smoke-testing.md is a "runbook"
  (procedure / checklist). Title parallel ("Cowork-side
  operational note" vs "Cowork-side operational runbook") signals
  the intended division of labor. Convention going forward.

  No reviewer subagents required (doc-only, no PII, no SQL, no
  routes; permission-by-omission framing). §2A self-merge.

CATCHES (Cowork-side and Claude Code-side observations worth banking)

  1. CC discipline: surface-rather-than-silently-execute moves.
     Two examples this session:
     - Caught my own bash redirect-order bug pre-execution
       (`2>&1 > /tmp/B.bin` would have produced an empty
       B.bin because bash processes redirects left-to-right
       and `2>&1` snapshots fd1's previous destination; the
       correct order is `> /tmp/B.bin 2>&1`). Surfaced before
       running rather than silently producing an empty
       capture and a misleading shasum comparison.
     - Self-corrected the audit count from "3 occurrences of
       the prospect's school name" to "2 occurrences" with
       receipts after a re-grep. Surfaced the count error
       rather than quietly tightening it. The third candidate
       I had vaguely flagged as "indirectly via Followup #11
       reference" turned out not to exist on re-inspection.
     Six sessions running with this discipline. Worth banking
     as load-bearing pattern.

  2. §2A self-merge framing sharpened. CC's audit on PR #37
     produced the more precise framing: "permission-by-
     omission, §4B cleanliness as the load-bearing
     precondition." Used verbatim in PR bodies for #37, #38,
     #39. Going forward this is the canonical framing for
     chore/activities-log and docs-only self-merge PRs;
     "chore/activities-log self-merge convention" is the
     less-precise shorthand and should be deprecated.

  3. Followup #34 diagnostic narrowed the original hypothesis.
     Session 40 attributed the password mismatch to "smart-
     quote substitution, em-dash transformation of trailing -
     in base64url passwords, or column-alignment-induced
     copy-paste error." NONE of these reproduced in the
     33-test diagnostic. The actual finding was much narrower
     (single-byte CR→LF substitution + a one-time line-level
     structural observation). The operational rule didn't
     change, but the explanation banked in the codebase is
     now sharper than the original hypothesis. Worth banking
     as a discipline note: a hypothesis stated in one session
     is not load-bearing until a follow-up diagnostic
     confirms it; the rule it justifies can stand even if the
     hypothesis is wrong, but the codebase explanation should
     be tightened to match the evidence.

  4. Genre split in docs/operational-notes/: "note" =
     postmortem / findings (cc-chat-rendering.md), "runbook" =
     procedure / checklist (smoke-testing.md). Title parallel
     ("Cowork-side operational note" vs "Cowork-side
     operational runbook") signals the intended division of
     labor. Convention going forward; future operational
     content should declare its genre via the title prefix.

  5. Lazy-render selection truncation discovered as a separate
     finding during the diagnostic execution itself. The
     operator's first attempt to copy CC's chat output for
     Point C captured only TESTs 1-18 of 33 because VS Code's
     chat panel renders lazily (off-screen content not in DOM
     until scrolled). Documented in cc-chat-rendering.md
     Finding 3 and smoke-testing.md Section 6. The diagnostic
     workflow itself surfaced a hazard the diagnostic was not
     looking for — a useful instance of the "instrument with
     enough surface area that adjacent bugs surface" pattern.

  6. Session 36 activities-log gap accepted permanently
     during P6. PR #28 (Session 36 underlying work — Humble
     ISD sandbox provisioned at tenant_id=9) is on main; the
     entry that documented it (commit 34a4a90 on
     chore/activities-log-session-36) never landed.
     Backfilling would have required a §4B audit on a
     pre-discipline-era draft. Discarded; gap accepted.
     Future session entries that need to reference the
     underlying work cite tenant_id=9 directly without
     expecting a corresponding activities entry to exist.

PR #6 — fix/resource-1-4-calendar-underscores → main (MERGED)

  Cosmetic markdown escape: the Resource 1.4 calendar section was
  rendering paired underscores as italic spans because the upstream
  Word-doc-export pipeline left bare underscores between calendar
  date tokens. The fix backslash-escapes the underscores so the
  markdown renderer treats them as literal characters.

  Disposition: MERGED via merge commit fdcb707 with --delete-branch
  on both local and remote. Self-merge per the standard convention
  for cosmetic / docs PRs that don't touch §4B or §5 surfaces (the
  PR contained a single-file markdown content change, no logic, no
  data model).

  Followup #35 (introduced earlier as the underscore-italics bug
  observation) CLOSED with this merge.
