SESSION: 2026-05-31T18:19:49-07:00
BRANCH:  chore/disciplineReferrals-input-hygiene
DISPOSITION: pr-merged:https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/188 (merge commit 60948c4c8d48207389873d4290e815450d52e310; §2A external human review APPROVED before merge; reviewDecision=APPROVED at merge time).
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TASK SUMMARY
  Three server-side input-hygiene hardenings on POST /api/discipline-
  referrals/ in routes/disciplineReferrals.js. Single commit, +49/-5,
  one file. No schema change, no migration, no new dependency, no
  behavior change on the happy path.

  1. Future incident_date guard. Reject incident_date > today (UTC)
     with a 400 before pool.connect(). Shape-regex (^YYYY-MM-DD$)
     first so junk values fail without Date-parser coercion. Both
     sides anchored to UTC midnight so the comparison aligns with
     the DB's COALESCE(..., CURRENT_DATE) on a UTC-running Render
     server. Same-day still passes (strict-> boundary).
       400 "Invalid incident_date"            on bad shape / NaN
       400 "incident_date cannot be in the future"  on future date
       Missing/empty falls through unchanged (DB COALESCE).

  2. staff_notes length cap. New constant STAFF_NOTES_MAX_LENGTH
     = 5000 and helper parseStaffNotes(raw) mirroring the existing
     parseAdminNotes helper. Replaces the inline trim+null block
     at the POST / call site. Closes the staff_notes side of the
     length-cap follow-up (admin_notes side already shipped in
     PR #184). parseStaffNotes deliberately accepts `undefined` as
     {ok, value:null} because POST / treats absent key as "no note"
     (the L2+ required-note check at the path-fork still gates on
     null/empty). parseAdminNotes deliberately rejects undefined
     because its PATCH callers must distinguish key-absent (preserve
     existing column) from key-present-null (explicit clear). The
     divergence is captured in a comment above the helper.
       400 "staff_notes must be a string"           on non-string
       400 "staff_notes exceeds maximum length"     on >5000 trim

  3. students lookup tenant tightening. Convert the same-tenant
     check from "fetch row, compare tenant_id in JS" to a WHERE-
     clause filter:
       BEFORE: SELECT grade, tenant_id FROM students WHERE id = $1
               then if (rows.length === 0 || rows[0].tenant_id !==
               targetTenantId) → 403 FORBIDDEN_BODY
       AFTER:  SELECT grade FROM students WHERE id = $1 AND
               tenant_id = $2 then if (rows.length === 0) →
               403 FORBIDDEN_BODY
     $2 = targetTenantId from resolveAndBindTargetTenant (§5 helper-
     consume, never inlined). Same 403 FORBIDDEN_BODY collapse;
     not-found and cross-tenant remain byte-identical. Projection
     narrowed to `grade` only. Mirrors the discipline_behaviors /
     discipline_consequences lookups already in this same function.

  Slight strictening on staff_notes happy path: a non-string body
  value (e.g. number, object, array, boolean) now 400s instead of
  silently coercing to null. Recorded as the FE-drift release note
  in the PR body.

FILES TOUCHED

  +/- lines    File                                   Reason
  ---------    -----------------------------------    --------------------------
  +49/-5       routes/disciplineReferrals.js          STAFF_NOTES_MAX_LENGTH +
                                                     parseStaffNotes helper;
                                                     future-date guard block in
                                                     POST /; students SELECT
                                                     tightened to WHERE id=$1
                                                     AND tenant_id=$2.

  Total: 1 file, +49/-5 (per git diff --stat main...HEAD).

MIGRATIONS RUN
  None. No schema change in this PR.

  tenant-isolation-auditor APPROVED (0 C / 0 H / 0 M / 0 L / 1 INFO).
  agentId: afd84fdb53e7e9081. One changed query audited (students
  lookup in POST /); zero added, zero removed. The change moves a
  tenant equality check from JS-post-fetch to SQL-WHERE — §5 posture
  STRENGTHENED. $2 confirmed bound to targetTenantId, which flows
  from resolveAndBindTargetTenant → resolveAccessibleTenantIds
  (middleware/resolveAccessibleTenantIds.js) — the §5 centralized
  helper. No inline `users.district_id` branching introduced;
  diff-wide grep for `district_id|user\.district` returns zero hits.
  Existence-disclosure still collapses: cross-tenant student_id and
  non-existent student_id both produce rows.length === 0 → identical
  403 FORBIDDEN_BODY. ROLLBACK swallow (S87 5a3cfd1 pattern) preserved
  on the cross-tenant 403 path with attribution comment intact.

TASK SUMMARY
  The S114 comprehensive remediation sprint. STRICT_STUDENT_ACCESS_PREDICATE
  was flipped to true in production at the start of the session — the
  teacher caseload predicate is now enforcing on every applyStudentAccessGate
  call site, not dev-only. With that as the live trust boundary, the
  session executed a top-to-bottom remediation of the cross-tenant /
  audit-trail surface:

    1) HIGH cross-tenant reads tier (R1-R5)
    2) HIGH cross-tenant writes (admin-templates)
    3) 504 process-vs-finalized-plan split (R4-A + R4-B)
    4) Repo-wide actor-field spoof sweep (FERPA audit trail)
    5) Two FE quality-of-life fixes (one bundled with the spoof sweep,
       one standalone for the silent-non-ok-response failure mode that
       had masked a prior regression)

  Ten work PRs landed back-to-back (#210-#219); this docs-only entry
  closes the session. Combined dev smoke total across the 10 work PRs:
  183/183 PASS (excluding #219 which was code-review-only per its
  FE/UX scope).

  #215  sec/admin-templates-writes-tenant-scoping  (HIGH closure)
        Re-audit caught what the prior sweep had under-classified as
        MEDIUM. Three plan-write handlers (PUT/DELETE /templates/:id
        /plan + POST /templates/:id/duplicate) were exploitable cross-
        tenant writes via body.tenant_id verbatim — any authenticated
        user including parents/teachers/students could rewrite
        another tenant's tenant_plan_template_overrides row or
        overwrite a tenant-owned intervention_templates row. Two-layer
        fix: new requireAdminTemplateRole (ELEVATED_ROLES allowlist —
        strict superset of FE Admin tab's isAdmin set, no regression)
        + Number()-coerce + accessible-set check on body.tenant_id,
        with tenantIdInt threaded through every downstream query AND
        the templateBelongsToTenant helper call. 30/30 smoke including
        parent + teacher role-gate denial rows and INSERT + UPSERT
        spoof attempts. A parallel re-audit of every live
        body/query-tenant_id write handler ran in tandem: re-confirmed
        the 6 prior-labeled "already correctly scoped" handlers
        (parentLinks × 2, districtAccess × 2, mtssCoordinators × 2)
        with explicit gate citations; surfaced 7 NEW Pattern E
        write surfaces (screener, mtssMeetings, students,
        prereferralForms, student504, csvImport, disciplineReferrals)
        all MEDIUM via the shared resolveAndBindTargetTenant helper.
  #216  sec/student504-finalized-plan-teacher-readable  (R4-B)
        Additive new endpoint GET /api/student-504/plans/student/
        :studentId. Gated by requireAuth + requireStudentReadAccess
        (teachers reach via caseload predicate now LIVE in prod;
        elevated staff and linked parents also covered). WHERE
        c.student_id=$1 AND c.tenant_id=$2 AND p.plan_status='active'
        — finalized only, never drafts. Minimized projection (5 keys:
        id, plan_status, effective_date, review_date, accommodations).
        Drops created_by + team_members.user_id to avoid staff-
        directory leak in the teacher tier. New FE component
        TeacherAccommodationsView; App.jsx conditional mount for
        role='teacher'. 12/12 smoke with STRICT_STUDENT_ACCESS_PREDICATE
        engaged. Privacy reviewer's framing: "net privacy reduction
        relative to the only realistic alternative (giving teachers
        Section504Tab)."
  #217  sec/student504-process-handlers-elevated-only  (R4-A)
        The real backend closure of the 504 process tier. Pre-fix
        used refuseParentRole (let teachers reach the process
        endpoints via curl/devtools); post-fix uses
        requireElevated504Role (ELEVATED_ROLES allowlist). Narrows 10
        process handlers covering active-form-set, cycles, consents,
        eligibility-determinations (incl. staff-only
        determination_notes), POST /plans drafts, and GET /plans/:id
        full staff bundle. Operator-confirmed: all 504-process staff
        in prod are coded as counselor/interventionist/school_admin;
        no FE caller blocked. R4-B teacher path intentionally
        untouched. 34/34 smoke (10 handlers × {teacher 403, parent
        403, elevated 200/201} + R4-B regression {teacher caseload
        200 + minimized projection} + cross-tenant 404 + no-auth 401).
        FE comment updates in 3 components for accuracy.
  #218  sec/actor-field-spoof-sweep
        Closes the FERPA audit-trail spoof family that PR-A
        (logged_by), PR-C (plan_completed_by), and PR #208
        (activated_by) had started. Phase 1 enumeration (read-only,
        operator-confirmed) identified exactly 3 SPOOF-FIXABLE
        sites repo-wide: tenant_plan_template_overrides.updated_by
        × 2 in adminTemplates (closes PR #215 WARN-1) and
        progress_notes.author_id (closes the file's own banked
        F-A followup that called the surface "impersonation"). 5
        LEGITIMATELY BODY-SOURCED fields verified and NOT touched
        (504 consent signatures × 4 + prereferral_forms.initiated_by
        — captured-at-write parties distinct from JWT identity by
        design). FE cleanup on 3 callers (silently-ignored body
        fields dropped). Spoof-proof smoke 10/10 PASS: caller=X
        submits actor=Y in body, DB stores X.id; covered INSERT,
        ON CONFLICT UPDATE × 2 sites, and FE-clean omit shape, plus
        cross-tenant sanity. Side-channel sweep independently
        confirmed by both security and privacy reviewers — no
        additional spoof-fixable actor columns remain in routes/.
  #219  fix/assignmentmanager-silent-non-ok-response  (FE/UX)
        The #203 invisibility fix. Pre-fix, addInterventionAssignment
        and removeInterventionAssignment silently swallowed non-ok
        HTTP responses — apiFetch never throws on 4xx/5xx, so the
        try/catch only caught network errors. A backend 400/500 fell
        through to return null with no user feedback. Now both
        handlers branch on response.ok and surface a generic
        hardcoded alert() — §4B-clean (no body echo, no err.message).
        Dev-side console.error tagging preserved + augmented with
        response.status int. Two reviewers approved (privacy + security;
        tenant-isolation skipped per FE/UX scope).

        WHERE ea_user_id=$1 AND student_id=$2 AND school_tenant_id=$3
        LIMIT 1. The third bind takes studentRow.tenant_id (already

        M041 FK3 guarantees the schema-layer trust property: no
        caseload row can exist whose school_tenant_id differs from

        branching — helper-consume only.

        resolveAccessibleTenantIds(req.user) BEFORE handler runs.

  canStaffAccessStudent per-record predicate. tenantId source
  is the path-validated integer ($1 binds twice), not a per-row
  column dereference. Outer s.tenant_id = $1 + inner EXISTS
  school_tenant_id = $1 are belt-and-suspenders against M041
  composite-FK weakening.
  Cascade-cleanup ordering on DELETE FROM users in both
  staffManagement.js and users.js: BEGIN → SET LOCAL
  app.actor_user_id → SELECT target / verify accessibility →
  explicit DELETE FROM ea_caseload_students (fires M041 trigger,
  captures actor via GUC, labels audit row 'cascade_user_delete')
  → DELETE FROM users → COMMIT. All on the same checked-out
  client per the M041 GUC contract. For legacy EAs (district_id
  IS NULL) the explicit DELETE is the only path that fires the
  trigger; for district EAs it is a 0-row no-op because the FK
  cascade would also fire later (also captured by trigger).
  Smoke confirmed: 2 audit rows written with action label
  'cascade_user_delete' and actor_user_id populated from the
  GUC on a legacy EA delete.
  tenant-isolation-auditor verdict: APPROVED, 0 CRITICAL, 0
  HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 0 LOW, 3 INFO.

DEV ENVIRONMENT NOTES
  M041 was NOT applied to the local dev DB pre-session — only
  the schema-foundation prod path had run. Operator authorized
  pre-flight + apply via the migration file's BEGIN/COMMIT
  atomic unit.
  Pre-flight blocked once on a CHECK violation: one dev-only
  legacy seed row carried a role value that fell outside the
  canonical 7-role universe inherited by M041's widened CHECK.
  Operator authorized Option A (UPDATE the offending row to a
  canonical role) to make M041's validating ADD CONSTRAINT
  apply cleanly. Row had 0 outbound references (no caseload,
  no user_school_access, no intervention_assignments). Dev-only
  drift, not prod (M041 applied prod-clean per banked memory).
  Server-boot DDL at server.js:438-444 re-DROPs and re-ADDs the
  CHECK with the 7-role universe on every server restart; M041's
  widened CHECK survives only until the next boot. The boot
  CHECK is NOT VALID, so existing EA rows survive
  revalidation-skip but new EA INSERTs after a boot will fail
  until the CHECK is re-widened. This is Followup #117
  (chore/retire-server-boot-ddl-for-users-role-check), banked
  pre-session and re-banked here as load-bearing for any
  multi-environment apply.
  Dev DB has no migration audit table (no schema_migrations /
  migrations / pgmigrations). The team applies migrations
  manually with no programmatic gating. Idempotency in each
  migration's header is the only safety net.

    /templates with a valid body would receive 201. Fix is a

    the top of each handler. Pre-existing M030-era code.

    LOW. The count of console.error('[wrapper-name]',
    err.message) sites in middleware/authorizeInterventionAccess
    .js is now 9. err.message from pg does not interpolate
    parameter values in the standard error path, but a project-
    wide safeLogError(tag, err) helper that strips known
    PII-shaped patterns would harden against future pg error
    variants or new error sources. Repo-wide hygiene item.

    missed POST /api/intervention-logs (the route had no PR-3
    Step 1 positive gate because it pre-dated the audit; it
    quietly admitted EA via applyStudentAccessGate's caseload
    branch). PR-4's exhaustive 22-route enumeration caught it.
    Lesson for future EA-or-similar role expansions:
    enumerate every write route from the router source, not a
    curated list. A curated NO-matrix is acceptable for a
    quick spot-check but never for the pre-merge sec-PR
    verification gate.

  followup banked S116. Pre-PR, POST /api/interventions/templates
  and DELETE /api/interventions/templates/:id were authenticated
  (router-level requireAuth at server.js mount) but lacked a role

TASK SUMMARY
  M042 student demographics — foundation schema layer for
  downstream discipline reporting. Adds 4 scalar columns to the
  students table (iep_flag / sec_504_flag / ell_flag as 3-state
  BOOLEAN, gender as VARCHAR(20) with a 4-value CHECK allowlist),
  1 multi-select child table (student_race_ethnicity) holding
  stable codes against a 7-code CHECK allowlist (2024 OMB SPD
  15 minima), and 1 append-only audit table
  (students_demographics_audit) mirroring the M039/M040/M041
  no-FK denormalized-actor doctrine. Three trigger functions
  with 4 bindings (AFTER INSERT + AFTER UPDATE on students;
  AFTER INSERT + AFTER DELETE on the race child table) capture
  initial-set and per-column-diff mutations.

  PR-A of a multi-PR sequence (schema -> CSV import -> backend
  writes -> FE). This entry covers PR-A only; PR-B / PR-C /
  PR-D are future units.

FILES TOUCHED
  migration-042-student-demographics.sql — NEW, 339 lines
    wrapped in a single BEGIN/COMMIT (no other files modified).

MIGRATIONS RUN
  M042 applied to prod via Render External PSQL one-shot
  command + \i migration-042-student-demographics.sql.
  Identical token sequence to dev apply: BEGIN ... DO ...
  5 ALTER TABLE ... 2 CREATE TABLE ... 3 CREATE INDEX ...
  3 CREATE FUNCTION ... 4 CREATE TRIGGER ... COMMIT.
  Pre-flight verified: students_id_tenant_unique precondition
  present; iep_flag column / student_race_ethnicity table /
  students_demographics_audit table all absent (no partial
  prior apply). Post-apply schema verification confirmed
  4 scalar columns, 2 new tables, 3 CHECK constraints,
  4 trigger bindings, the composite FK shape, and 6 indexes.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  Composite FK (student_id, tenant_id) -> students(id, tenant_id)
  on the race child table is the §5 anchor; cross-tenant child
  rows impossible by construction. Verified by smoke (cross-
  tenant INSERT returns 23503 foreign_key_violation in both
  dev and prod). students_demographics_audit carries denormalized
  tenant_id NOT NULL plus nullable district_id (no FKs per
  FERPA §99.32 retention). Every trigger-body INSERT sources
  tenant from NEW/OLD of the firing row, never from external
  input; v_district lookup is same-tenant self-lookup of
  tenants.district_id with an EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
  v_district := NULL fallback. All three trigger functions
  run SECURITY INVOKER (no privilege escalation surface).
  tenant-isolation-auditor verdict: APPROVED, 0 CRITICAL,
  0 WARN, 5 INFO (all banking-for-PR-B/C).

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  Sensitive demographic PII surface (race / IEP / 504 / ELL +
  gender). Smallest-viable shape: 3 BOOLEANs, 1 short-code
  VARCHAR, 1 normalized child table with a 7-code CHECK
  allowlist. No DOB, no address, no parent identifiers, no
  free-text. Race codes are opaque without app-side label
  map (privacy-improving — labels live in app constants,
  deferred to PR-B with first consumer).
  students_demographics_audit captures old_value / new_value
  from OLD.<col> / NEW.<col> / NEW.category only — no row
  echo, no body interpolation, no SQL fragment capture.

  dimension. Audit table has zero FKs per FERPA §99.32

      legible at the call site.

  the "set at import" semantic). Tenant scoping is parameter-driven

SESSION: 2026-06-06T10:12:42-07:00
BRANCH:  feat/student-demographics-writes
DISPOSITION: pr-opened:https://github.com/mmarronetravels-sudo/tiertrak/pull/233
             (PR stays in REVIEW_REQUIRED until §2A human review lands;
              this entry rides on chore/activities-log-pr233)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TASK SUMMARY
  PR-C from the M042 student-demographics multi-PR plan. Extended the
  authenticated POST and PUT handlers on routes/students.js to accept
  the five new M042 demographic fields (iep_flag, sec_504_flag,
  ell_flag, gender on the students scalar columns; race/ethnicity as
  an array of codes on the student_race_ethnicity child table). Both
  handlers now run inside the M040 GUC writer contract so the M042
  AFTER INSERT/UPDATE triggers on students and student_race_ethnicity
  capture the real actor_user_id into students_demographics_audit.
  Companion hoist: the three CSV sanitizers moved out of
  routes/csvImport.js into constants/studentDemographics.js as a pure
  byte-for-byte move; three JSON-shape sibling sanitizers added in
  the same module for the route layer. CSV import behavior unchanged
  (PR-B redaction shape preserved).

FILES TOUCHED
  constants/studentDemographics.js — pure-move hoist of three CSV
    sanitizers from routes/csvImport.js (verified byte-identical
    against main); three new JSON-shape sanitizers
    (sanitizeBooleanFlagJson, sanitizeGenderJson,
    sanitizeRaceEthnicityArray) added alongside; exports widened.
  routes/csvImport.js — three module-local sanitizer declarations
    removed; replaced by destructured import from
    constants/studentDemographics.js plus a 5-line pointer comment.
    All other csvImport behavior unchanged.
  routes/students.js — POST and PUT rewritten on pool.connect() +
    BEGIN/set_config('app.actor_user_id', String(actorId), true)/
    COMMIT. Five new demographic fields sanitized and written to
    students (4 scalars) and student_race_ethnicity (multi-select).
    PUT uses hasOwnProperty + SQL CASE WHEN preserve-on-omit on every
    nullable demographic field. PUT child reconciliation runs SELECT
    ... FOR UPDATE on student_race_ethnicity inside the transaction,
    diffs current vs desired, INSERTs adds and DELETEs removes. The
    PUT UPDATE WHERE was tightened from tenant_id = ANY(accessible[])
    to tenant_id = $studentTenantId (the single integer from the
    pre-flight student lookup). Non-23505 catches now redact to a
    generic operator-facing string; error code + constraint are
    logged server-side only.
  Follow-up commit on the same branch added the M040-style actor-id
    guard at the top of POST and PUT (per security-reviewer LOW-1):
      const actorId = Number(req.user.id);
      if (!Number.isInteger(actorId) || actorId <= 0) {
        console.error('[students:post|put]', 'invalid req.user.id from JWT');
        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'Server error' });
      }
    String(actorId) is bound at both set_config sites (was
    String(req.user.id) in the initial commit).

MIGRATIONS RUN
  none (M042 applied in the prior session/PR; this session is
  app-code only)

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  Three new write surfaces audited:
    - Parent students INSERT in POST. tenant_id is $1, bound to the
      per-request targetTenantId from resolveAndBindTargetTenant(req);
      the helper validates body-supplied target_tenant_id against
      resolveAccessibleTenantIds(req.user) and 403s on out-of-set
      before any INSERT.
    - Per-code student_race_ethnicity INSERT in POST (loop). tenant_id
      bound to the same targetTenantId scalar; composite FK
      (student_id, tenant_id) -> students(id, tenant_id) is the
      structural backstop.
    - Parent students UPDATE in PUT. WHERE clause binds tenant_id =
      $studentTenantId, the single integer from the pre-flight lookup
      after the resolveAccessibleTenantIds JS-layer guard. studentTenantId
      cannot hold an out-of-scope tenant; the guard sits between SELECT
      and assignment with no intervening statements.
    - Child reconciliation SELECT FOR UPDATE / INSERT / DELETE on
      student_race_ethnicity in PUT. Every predicate carries the same
      studentTenantId scalar.
  No tenant_id = ANY(accessible) widening at any write — every write
  in this PR binds a single integer. Composite FK backstop confirmed
  at migration-042-student-demographics.sql line 87-90.
  tenant-isolation-auditor verdict on the first commit: APPROVED
  (0 CRITICAL / 0 WARN / 8 INFO verifications). The LOW-1 follow-up
  commit only adds a JS-layer integer guard on req.user.id and does
  not change any SQL.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched (write path): iep_flag, sec_504_flag, ell_flag,
    gender (students scalar columns); category (student_race_ethnicity
    child table). All extra-sensitive FERPA per CLAUDE.md §4B; subject
    to COPPA for any student under 13 and to applicable state student
    privacy laws.
  Logging audit: two new console.error sites in the POST and PUT
    wrapper-catch path log only error.code and error.constraint — no
    error.message, no error.detail, no row payload, no PII. The LOW-1
    follow-up commit added two more console.error sites for the
    invalid-req.user.id-from-JWT path; both log the literal tag and
    the literal 'invalid req.user.id from JWT' string only.
  Error-body audit: three new JSON sanitizers cite column name + valid
    set, NEVER echo offending input. Smoke-replayed with a SQL-injection
    probe, a canary marker, a student-id-shaped string, and an object
    payload — zero echoes. The two 23505 catches (POST and PUT) use
    static literal strings — no interpolation of external_id, no
    interpolation of the race/ethnicity code. The non-23505 catch
    blocks return a generic operator-facing string.
  Trigger-owned audit table: the app NEVER writes to
    students_demographics_audit; the M042 AFTER INSERT / AFTER UPDATE
    triggers own it. The GUC writer contract ensures actor_user_id is
    captured on every POST and PUT path; CSV import retains the "set
    at import = NULL actor" semantic per M042 doctrine.
  CSV sanitizer hoist: byte-identical move against main; the PR-B
    redaction shape and three-state semantic preserved verbatim.
    Re-ran PR-B smoke (b) against the hoisted sanitizers — bad token,
    bad gender code, and bad race code each produce a redacted error
    that does NOT contain the offending input.
  Cross-tenant risk: none identified. See TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW.
  §4B grep (narrow + wide) against diff vs main: 0 matches across the
    full diff, the PR description body, and the commit messages.
  privacy-reviewer verdict on the first commit: APPROVED (0 CRITICAL
  / 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 0 LOW with code change recommended; 2 LOW
  banked as cosmetic / followup-bank items).

SESSION: 2026-06-06T10:59:10-07:00 (CORRIGENDUM to the prior PR #233 entry)
BRANCH:  chore/activities-log-pr233-corrigendum
DISPOSITION: docs-lane corrigendum; the PR-C feature work is already on main
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CORRIGENDUM TARGET
  The immediately-prior PR #233 entry recorded DISPOSITION as
  "pr-opened / REVIEW_REQUIRED" and OPEN ITEMS as work the §2A
  reviewer must drive before merge. Both statements were stale at
  the moment the entry was written.

WHAT WAS ACTUALLY TRUE AT WRITE TIME
  PR #233 had already merged to main at commit 2d960a2 at
  2026-06-06T17:11:43Z, and the auto-deploy on the main branch had
  already fired. The corrected disposition is:

    DISPOSITION (corrected): MERGED + deployed
    Merge commit:            2d960a2
    Schema change:           none (M042 was already live on main; PR-C
                             is app-code only on routes/students.js +
                             constants/studentDemographics.js +
                             routes/csvImport.js)

PROCESS NOTE
  The merge was a bare self-approve by the operational account: the
  review body is the empty string, and the elapsed time between
  approve submission and merge click was ~6 seconds. Per CLAUDE.md
  §2A, a §2A human review must carry substance, not just a
  reviewDecision flip. The §2A substance gate was not met at merge
  time. The PR description's NOT-MERGEABLE-UNTIL block (three
  dev-DB integration tests under strict mode) had no evidence of
  being run pre-merge in any artifact attached to the PR — no
  review-body narrative, no PR comment, no commit, no CI run.

REMEDIATION (executed post-merge on dev)
  All three NOT-MERGEABLE-UNTIL invariants from the PR description
  were exercised against the dev DB on localhost with
  STRICT_STUDENT_ACCESS_PREDICATE=true, against the real route
  handlers via HTTP through the full requireAuth + CSRF + role-gate
  stack. Seeded two synthetic school-typed tenant rows (tenant 1,
  tenant 2), one school_admin user per tenant, one student row per
  tenant. All seed data uses integer ids + placeholder strings only;
  no real names, no slugs, no SIS identifiers. All seed rows torn
  down inside the same script run.

  Test 1 — cross-tenant write denial.
    Acting as the tenant-1 school_admin:
      PUT /api/students/<tenant-2 student id>
        → 404 "Student not found"
      POST /api/students with target_tenant_id = tenant-2 id
        → 403 "Not authorized for target tenant"
    Post-test row counts in tenant 2:
      students row count             : 1   (the unmodified seed row)
      iep_flag on that students row  : NULL (PUT payload was rejected)
      student_race_ethnicity rows    : 0
      students_demographics_audit rows for the tenant-2 student : 0
    Verdict: PASS — zero writes to tenant 2.

  Test 2 — audit actor capture (GUC reaches the trigger).
    Acting as the tenant-1 school_admin (acting user id = U1.id):
      POST /api/students with 4 demographic fields set
        → 201 + new students row id S3
      PUT  /api/students/<S3> with 3 demographic fields changed
        → 200
    students_demographics_audit row count for S3 : 7
      4 initial-set rows from POST (iep_flag, gender, race_ethnicity
      x 2 categories) + 3 delta rows from PUT (iep_flag flip,
      sec_504_flag new, race_ethnicity 1-category remove).
    actor_user_id on every one of those 7 rows : equals U1.id
    Final student_race_ethnicity for S3 : 1 row (the kept category).
    Verdict: PASS — the M040 GUC writer contract delivers the actor
    integer to the M042 AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE triggers on both
    students and student_race_ethnicity.

  Test 3 — rollback atomicity on mid-transaction child failure.
    Installed a temporary BEFORE INSERT trigger on
    student_race_ethnicity that RAISEs for one specific test
    students.id; left every other student write path unaffected.
    PUT /api/students/<that test student id> with a payload that
    mutates 8 fields including a race_ethnicity add → child INSERT
    raises mid-transaction.
      Response   : 500 "Failed to update student" (generic, redacted)
      Parent row : byte-identical pre/post (every column, including
                   updated_at, matches)
      student_race_ethnicity child rows : 0 added, 0 removed
      students_demographics_audit rows  : 0 added (count unchanged)
    Verdict: PASS — ROLLBACK is clean across parent UPDATE, child
    INSERT, and the trigger-emitted audit rows.

  After the test run, teardown removed every test row and dropped
  the test trigger and test function. Post-teardown query confirms:
    leftover tenants     : 0
    leftover users       : 0
    leftover students    : 0
    leftover race_ethnicity rows : 0
    leftover audit rows  : 0
    leftover test trigger: 0
    leftover test function: 0

  No app code modified. No revert. No merge. Working tree clean
  except for three pre-existing untracked files unrelated to this
  session.

NET
  The merged PR-C code behaves correctly under all three
  NOT-MERGEABLE-UNTIL invariants. The audit-trail gap on the
  pre-merge §2A review (bare approve, empty body, ~6 second window,
  no evidence of integration tests) is recorded here as a process
  observation, not as a code issue. No code change is being
  proposed off the back of this remediation.

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS
  Process:
    - §2A reviewer protocol: when a NOT-MERGEABLE-UNTIL block names
      integration tests, an APPROVED review must carry a non-empty
      body that quotes the test results or links to a CI/test
      artifact. A bare approve does not meet the §2A substance gate
      even when the merge button is clicked by the reviewer. Worth
      writing this expectation into CLAUDE.md §2A's "what counts as
      a review" subsection if it is not already explicit there.
  Code:
    - All three PR #233 review findings still stand:
      privacy-reviewer LOW-1 (comment at students.js:737-739 does
      not document the TOCTOU mitigation), privacy-reviewer LOW-2
      (the log-redaction-hygiene-primitive followup), security-
      reviewer LOW-2 (params.id 22P02 → 500, Followup #76),
      security-reviewer INFO (sanitizeRaceEthnicityArray length cap,
      bundle with disciplineReferrals notes-length-cap). LOW-1 from
      security-reviewer (actor-id integer guard) was already applied
      pre-merge in commit e54d958 on the feature branch.

NEXT SESSION SHOULD

  fields) is the natural next unit. Treat the PR #233 NOT-MERGEABLE-

SCOPE
  FE half of M042 student demographics. Surfaces the four scalar
  fields (iep_flag, sec_504_flag, ell_flag, gender) in the student
  create form, the student edit form, and the CSV-import description
  panel. Race/ethnicity (the fifth M042 dimension) was intentionally
  phased to PR-E and kept entirely out of PR-D — including the CSV-
  description text — to avoid a half-built "settable at create but
  invisible on edit" state. PR-E is gated on a precursor BE PR that
  widens GET /students to ARRAY_AGG the race_ethnicity codes.

  Files changed:
    frontend/src/App.jsx                          | +265 / -16
    frontend/src/constants/studentDemographics.js | new, +56
    -------------------------------------------------------
    2 files changed, 305 insertions(+), 16 deletions(-)

PRESERVE-ON-OMIT (highest-risk §4B correctness invariant)
  An Unknown demographic flag (NULL in the DB) must NEVER be silently
  coerced to FALSE on a no-op save. The end-to-end contract:

    startEditStudent → reads student row from GET /students payload
    → normalizeFlag()/normalizeGender() return null for anything
      non-canonical (including DB NULL itself)
    → setStudentForm() loads form
    → setInitialDemographics() captures snapshot at the same render
      tick
    → user opens form; Unknown radio rendered checked via the
      `value === null` predicate (App.jsx in PR-D)
    → user saves without touching demographic widgets
    → handleUpdateStudent builds PUT body and, for each of the four
      demographic keys, checks studentForm[k] !== initialDemographics[k];
      identical snapshots → key OMITTED from the body
    → BE PUT handler (routes/students.js, unchanged in this PR)
      Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty('iep_flag') = false → CASE WHEN
      gate fires the ELSE branch → column preserved
    → AFTER UPDATE trigger does not fire on unchanged column (or fires
      a no-delta row, but the demographic-audit table only writes
      rows for actual deltas).

  Net invariant: opening an Unknown-flag student and saving untouched
  emits ZERO demographic keys on the wire and the DB column stays
  NULL. "We don't know" stays distinct from "we said no" end-to-end.

LIVE SMOKE — OPERATOR-DRIVEN (NOT EXECUTED CLI-SIDE)
  The Unknown-flag round-trip smoke requires:
    (a) An authenticated browser session as a real role with
        student-edit access on a real school-tenant — CLI cannot do.
    (b) A psql session against the production Render DB to confirm
        the row state pre- and post-save — operator workflow per the
        TierTrak DB-workflow standard (Render psql + \i; no in-
        browser Shell).

  Smoke script for operator to run after the production alias rolls
  to the new bundle:

    PRE
      Hard-refresh tiertrak.scholarpathsystems.org and confirm the
      new bundle hash in devtools Network panel differs from
      index-D6pLqcFU.js (the pre-PR-D hash). If it still matches,
      wait for CDN rollover and retry.

      Pick a student row where the four demographic columns are
      all NULL:

        SELECT id, iep_flag, sec_504_flag, ell_flag, gender
          FROM students
         WHERE iep_flag IS NULL
           AND sec_504_flag IS NULL
           AND ell_flag IS NULL
           AND gender IS NULL
         LIMIT 1;

    CONTROL — untouched-save
      Open the student's Edit modal. Confirm the rendered radios for
      IEP, Section 504, and ELL all show Unknown selected (NOT No).
      Confirm Gender select shows "Not recorded". Save without
      touching any demographic widget.

      In devtools Network panel, inspect the PUT
      /api/students/<id> request body. EXPECT: zero demographic keys
      in the JSON body (no iep_flag, no sec_504_flag, no ell_flag,
      no gender). The body should contain only the non-demographic
      fields: first_name, last_name, grade, tier, area,
      secondary_area, risk_level.

      Re-query the DB:
        SELECT iep_flag, sec_504_flag, ell_flag, gender
          FROM students WHERE id = <id>;
      EXPECT: all four columns still NULL. Zero new rows in the
      demographic audit table for this student.id.

    EXPLICIT-CHANGE — proves the diff path still emits when touched
      Re-open the Edit modal for the same student. Click the IEP
      "Yes" radio. Leave the other three widgets untouched. Save.
      EXPECT in PUT body: { ..., "iep_flag": true }  — ONLY iep_flag,
      no sec_504_flag, no ell_flag, no gender.
      EXPECT in DB: iep_flag = true; the other three still NULL;
      exactly one new audit row for iep_flag.

  Outcome: NOT CAPTURED IN THIS ENTRY. Operator-driven smoke.
  If smoke fails after CDN rollover, escalate via a corrigendum
  entry following the PR #233 corrigendum precedent in the prior
  activities.txt block.

DEVIATIONS CALLED OUT
  Three deviations recorded explicitly in the PR #236 description
  and commit message:

    1. resetStudentForm gained `secondary_area: ''` as part of the
       reset shape — a latent FE-state bug fix where canceling an
       edit after touching secondary_area could leak stale state
       into the next opened form. FE-state-only. Does NOT touch the
       backend secondary_area drift (DR-1); the wire shape on PUT
       for secondary_area is unchanged from main.

    2. Introduced a small `DemographicFlagRadioGroup` helper
       component at module scope rather than inlining 12 identical
       radio blocks across two forms. Audit said "small helper
       extraction"; called out explicitly so reviewer sees it.

    3. Race/ethnicity is omitted from the CSV-import FE description
       text as well as the forms (conservative read of "out of PR-D
       entirely"). Known UX gap: downloading the BE-supplied CSV
       template returns a race_ethnicity column the FE doesn't yet
       document. Lands with PR-E.

SESSION: 2026-06-06T22:00-2026-06-06T22:35Z (PR-E Piece 1 — race/ethnicity GET-payload widening, BE)

SCOPE
  Backend precursor for the FE race/ethnicity edit form (PR-E Piece 2,
  next session). Widens GET /api/students/tenant/:tenantId (all four
  role branches) and GET /api/students/:studentId with a correlated
  ARRAY_AGG subquery on student_race_ethnicity. The new column is an
  alphabetized varchar[] per row, COALESCEd to ARRAY[]::varchar[] so
  the field is always an array and never null. Subquery is bound to
  the outer student row via (sre.student_id = s.id AND sre.tenant_id =
  s.tenant_id) — belt-and-suspenders even though M042's composite FK
  (student_id, tenant_id) REFERENCES students(id, tenant_id) already
  makes cross-tenant child rows structurally impossible.

  Files changed:
    routes/students.js | 40 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-----
    -------------------------------------------------------
    1 file changed, 35 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

  Five identical subquery insertions across:
    - list elevated branch          (1 site)
    - list parent branch            (1 site)
    - list education_assistant      (1 site)
    - list staff/teacher branch     (1 site)
    - single-student edit-hydrate   (1 site)

  option in the GitHub UI rather than squash. By the time the author-
  side `gh pr merge 238 --squash` ran, GitHub returned "already
  merged."

      different in git log.

  admin force-push on main. Recording the deviation here is the

LIVE SMOKE — OPERATOR-DRIVEN
  Authenticated curl against prod requires an httpOnly session cookie
  the CLI side does not possess. Per CLAUDE.md / CC-memory
  feedback-post-rotation-self-smoke, "deploy reports live" is not
  sufficient proof of rollover — the new code must be observed
  serving a request that exercises the new payload key.

  Operator executed the smoke against prod with their own browser-
  captured auth_token cookie. Test was the rollover-proof variant
  (the alphabetized-2-row variant could not be run in prod because
  no race_ethnicity rows exist there yet):

    A. GET /api/students/:studentId
       EXPECT: race_ethnicity key present in response body; value [].
       RESULT: PASS — key present, value [].

    B. GET /api/students/tenant/:tenantId
       EXPECT: race_ethnicity key present on the target student's
               row in the array response; value [].
       RESULT: PASS — key present, value [].

  Rollover proof: the pre-merge code did NOT emit a race_ethnicity
  key at all on either endpoint; the post-merge code emits it on
  every row. Observing the key in the live response body confirms
  the deployed bundle is the new bundle, independent of Render's
  green-check.

  Alphabetized-2-row case was verified in-branch (local dev curl
  dance: 0 rows → []; 1 row → ['WHITE']; 2 rows → ['HISP','WHITE']
  alphabetized; post-cleanup → []). Prod re-verification of the
  array shape is deferred to first natural use after Piece 2 ships.

    structurally indexed.

    selectivity): planner switches to student_race_ethnicity_unique
    Index Scan as predicted. Index Cond on student_id; 3 actual rows
    touched; 0.013ms execution time. Confirms the UNIQUE index will
    be selected at production scale.

SCOPE
  Frontend control for the OMB SPD-15 (2024 revised) seven race/
  ethnicity checkboxes. Lands on three surfaces:

    1. AddStudentForm — full-width fieldset below the demographics
       grid; seven checkboxes iterating RACE_ETHNICITY_CODES in the
       declared SPD-15 order; toggle handler runs the value through
       canonicalRace (sort + dedupe + allowlist-filter) before
       setState.
    2. Edit-student form — same fieldset, same iteration order, bound
       to studentForm + setStudentForm; submit-time set-diff at
       handleUpdateStudent emits race_ethnicity ONLY when the canonical
       form differs from the snapshot captured at startEditStudent.
    3. CSV-import description panel — adds a 9th <li> for
       race_ethnicity with the seven codes and the ';' separator;
       extends the italic note to cover "blank means none recorded".

  Files changed:
    frontend/src/App.jsx                          | 108 +++++++++++++++++++++++++-
    frontend/src/constants/studentDemographics.js |  16 ++--
    -------------------------------------------------------
    2 files changed, 117 insertions(+), 7 deletions(-)

  Render iteration is INTENTIONALLY DISTINCT from the wire-canonical
  sort. RACE_ETHNICITY_CODES is declared in OMB SPD-15 (2024 revised)
  order (AIAN → ASIAN → BLACK → HISP → MENA → NHPI → WHITE) and the
  checkbox group iterates it directly. canonicalRace sorts
  alphabetically + dedupes + filters through the FE allowlist for the
  wire form and the snapshot set-diff only — it is never called inside
  a render iteration. The declared-order intent is locked with a
  comment at the constant declaration so a future reordering fails
  review against the comment, not against silent regression.

  Preserve-on-omit (the user-visible invariant):
    - startEditStudent captures snap.race_ethnicity =
      canonicalRace(student.race_ethnicity) — alphabetized, deduped,
      allowlist-filtered.
    - studentForm.race_ethnicity is a defensive copy
      [...snap.race_ethnicity] so in-place mutation can't bleed into
      the snapshot.
    - handleUpdateStudent: if
      JSON.stringify(canonicalRace(form)) === JSON.stringify(snap),
      the key is OMITTED entirely from the PUT body.
    - BE Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(req.body,
      'race_ethnicity') gate at routes/students.js:764 (unchanged
      from PR-E Piece 1) preserves the student_race_ethnicity rows.

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS
  - Followup — apply-block trailing-SELECTs swallowed by \i: the apply
    block's post-apply verification SELECTs (column / constraint / index
    presence + GROUP BY count) ended up on the same line as the \i
    include command in the operator's psql session, so psql parsed them
    as extra arguments to \i and discarded them (emitting "\i: extra
    argument ... ignored" notices in the session output). They never
    executed — this is the same-line-as-\i failure mode, NOT a silent-
    inside-include failure. Lesson for future migration apply blocks:
    post-apply verification SELECTs MUST be separate top-level statements
    on their own lines, never on the same line as \i, or psql swallows
    them. Documentation-only workflow fix; no code or schema change.

  - Followup — sec/exit-reason-route-allowlist (PR 2 obligation, banked
    from privacy-reviewer INFO on PR #251): when the EOY roll-up feature
    PR wires writes to exit_reason, it MUST enforce a route-layer
    allowlist before INSERT/UPDATE, MUST NOT allow exit_reason to absorb
    free-form PII, and MUST NOT echo exit_reason into log lines or error
    bodies. Mirrors archived_reason route-validation precedent.

  - Followup — chore/privacy-review-m044-fields docs PR (banked from
    privacy-reviewer INFO on PR #251): PRIVACY_REVIEW.md does not yet
    enumerate enrollment_status / exit_reason / exit_date. Doc update

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS
  - Followup — apply-block post-\i SELECTs swallowed (RECURRENCE;
    same failure mode flagged on the M044 apply): for the second
    consecutive migration, the standalone post-apply verification
    SELECTs in the apply block were parsed by psql as extra
    arguments to the preceding \i meta-command and discarded. The
    in-file verification SELECTs inside the migration's BEGIN/COMMIT
    block still fired and provided the only eyeball confirmation.
    The M044 follow-up's prescription ("on their own lines, never
    on the same line as \i") was not sufficient — the recurrence
    proves psql's \i argument parsing can still absorb trailing
    statements depending on prompt/buffer state when issued in the
    same paste. DURABLE FIX (strengthened, operator workflow only,
    no code or schema change): post-apply verification SELECTs MUST
    be issued as a fully separate psql command — separate paste,
    separate execution from the \i line — never bundled into the
    same block or paste. Adoption rule for all future migration
    apply blocks starting now. Belt-and-suspenders option: keep the
    migration's in-file verification SELECTs inside BEGIN/COMMIT
    (already standard since M042) so post-COMMIT standalone
    verification is a check, not the only signal.

  - Followup — PRIVACY_REVIEW.md M045 doc subsection (banked from
    privacy-reviewer WARN-1 on PR #253): no entry yet for either
    new audit table. Sibling-shape to followup #120 (M031) and
    project_followup_pr_d_privacy_review_doc_m042 (M042). LOW
    priority, doc-only; should land in PR 2's window so the M045
    subsection can describe the full read/write contract including
    preview_snapshot_hash semantics, not just the schema shape.

  - Followup — preview_snapshot_hash salting + equality
    documentation (banked from privacy-reviewer WARN-2 on PR #253):
    the M045 doc subsection above MUST explicitly state (a) the
    hash is HMAC-SHA-256 keyed by PREVIEW_TOKEN_SECRET per PR 2's
    locked decision (not plain SHA-256), (b) that hash equality
    across runs is intended metadata ("same cohort previewed
    twice") and is not a §4B re-identification surface, and (c)
    that the hash is never decoded back to student_ids on either
    side of the wire.

  - Followup — preview_snapshot_hash format CHECK as defense-in-
    depth (banked from security-reviewer WARN-1 on PR #253):
    VARCHAR(64) accepts any string ≤64 chars; a future second
    writer could land a truncated or mis-encoded digest. Either
    add CHECK (preview_snapshot_hash ~ '^[0-9a-f]{64}$') alongside
    PR 2 route validation, or document the single-writer invariant
    in PR 2 with a route file/line citation.

  - Followup — exit_reason DB-layer CHECK as defense-in-depth
    (banked from security-reviewer WARN-2 on PR #253): exit_reason
    has no DB CHECK in this PR (mirrors archived_reason precedent).
    A future second writer (backfill, ops script, sandbox seed)
    could land an off-allowlist value into an audit table that
    outlives application code by FERPA §99.32 design. Add either a
    DB CHECK once PR 2 finalizes the allowlist, or document the
    single-writer invariant.

  - Followup — PR 2 tenant-isolation carryover watch-items (banked
    from tenant-isolation-auditor INFO on PR #253): INSERTs into
    student_grade_rollup_runs MUST source target_school_tenant_id +
    district_id from resolveAccessibleTenantIds(req.user) — never
    req.body. INSERTs into student_grade_rollup_event_rows MUST
    source school_tenant_id + district_id from the parent run
    header within the same transaction — never from request input
    or per-student lookup. preview_snapshot_hash HMAC payload MUST
    include target_school_tenant_id so a school-A preview cannot
    replay against school-B. Every read endpoint MUST WHERE-scope
    on target_school_tenant_id / school_tenant_id. Undo MUST verify
    actor's accessible-tenant set against the run header's
    target_school_tenant_id before reversal.

    project_pr_236_external_human_review_attribution: PR #253 was
    again approved through the shared scholapathsystems COLLABORATOR

  0 CRITICAL / 0 WARN / 4 INFO. All five §5 contract criteria verified

SECURITY REVIEW
  Initial security-reviewer subagent on diff: APPROVED W/NOTES,
    0 CRITICAL / 2 WARN / 3 INFO.
  Both WARNs fixed in-branch before human review and re-reviewed:
    WARN-1 (HMAC verify Buffer encoding): explicit 'utf8' on both
      Buffer.from calls in verifyPreviewToken. Semantically equivalent
      to prior default; intent now load-bearing in source.
    WARN-2 (/undo double-undo race): pre-transaction pool.query header
      SELECT removed; header SELECT now runs inside the transaction
      with FOR UPDATE so concurrent /undo on the same runId serialize
      on the row lock. ROLLBACK on all three early-return branches
      (row missing / scope mismatch / already undone). Final header
      UPDATE adds `AND undone_at IS NULL` + `rowCount !== 1 → ROLLBACK
      + 409` guard (belt-and-suspenders against a future refactor
      dropping FOR UPDATE).
  Security-reviewer delta re-review on the WARN-fix commit
    (55da9b7c874f282dc9dc1a174b37f88092fdc925): APPROVED,
    0 CRITICAL / 0 WARN / 0 INFO. Both fixes verified clean; no
    regressions; tenant isolation unchanged.
  HMAC verify confirmed using crypto.timingSafeEqual with explicit
    length-gate; single 401 body for every signature/format failure (no
    oracle).
  Two INFO findings banked as project memory: own pg pool vs shared
    pool (chore/pg-pool-consolidation candidate); per-child INSERT loop
    UNNEST batch (perf/student-grade-rollup-batch-insert candidate).

  row in the districts table. No tenant attachment, no first-admin user, no

  + the auth_mode CHECK is already in production (M028 + M034), so no
  migration in this PR.

FILES TOUCHED
  routes/operatorDistricts.js — new route handler (+68 lines), single POST /
    that mirrors the existing operator-only route conventions used by
    routes/tenants.js: pool with conditional SSL, router.use(requireAuth,
    platformAdminOnly), 23514 / 23505 PG-code redirects, redacted 500 with a
    bracketed scope tag.
  server.js — 2-line addition: route import + mount at /api/operator/districts.
    Mount sits adjacent to the existing /api/districts mounts so the URL
    namespace is co-located; prefix-disjoint per the tenant-isolation audit.

MIGRATIONS RUN
  none — districts table from M028 and auth_mode NOT NULL + CHECK from M034
  are already in production. This PR adds no schema.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  Endpoint writes to the districts table only (the top-level parent in the
  layered tenant model). No reads or writes against tenants, users,
  user_school_access, students, staff, or any school-scoped table. The
  middleware chain is requireAuth -> platformAdminOnly, with
  resolveAccessibleTenantIds deliberately omitted: operators have no
  user_school_access rows by construction, and the endpoint creates a
  top-level entity with no FK that could be cross-wired. Body destructure
  is strict (only name + auth_mode are read); INSERT column list is static.
  tenant-isolation-auditor on diff: APPROVED, 0 CRITICAL / 0 WARN / 3 INFO.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched: none. The handler reads only the districts.name field
    (a legal-entity name for a local education agency, not an individual
    identifier) and districts.auth_mode (a policy enum). No individual
    identifier is read, written, logged, or transmitted.
  Logging audit: one new console.error site, scope-tagged, emits err.message
    only to server logs. Response body in the 500 path is the static string
    'Server error' — err.message never reaches the wire. All five 4xx
    response bodies are static string literals; no caller input is echoed.
  Cross-tenant risk: none identified. No school-scoped table is read or
    written.
  privacy-reviewer on diff: APPROVED, 0 CRITICAL / 0 WARN / 3 INFO.
  §4B grep (narrow + wide) against added activities-entry lines vs
    origin/main: see VERIFICATION below.

VERIFICATION
  Static syntax check:
    node --check routes/operatorDistricts.js — OK.
    node --check server.js — OK.

    #73, package.json scripts.test is the placeholder echo + exit).
  Backend lint: not run; npm run lint is unconfigured (Followup #74).
  Curl smoke (untracked per Followup #94, off the work-PR branch):
    6 / 6 PASS — six cases exercised against a local server on port 3001
    with a freshly-seeded synthetic fixture + two test users (one made an
    operator via PLATFORM_ADMIN_USER_IDS override at boot, one not). Cases
    covered: unauthenticated -> 401, authed-non-operator -> 403, operator
    missing name -> 400, operator invalid auth_mode -> 400, operator valid
    fresh -> 201 with the RETURNING projection, operator same name -> 409
    via the best-effort pre-flight. Synthetic fixture rows + the row
    created by the endpoint during smoke are both torn down on trap EXIT.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

PR #262 commit OIDs at merge time:
  5b8abcae15f3cdb048e746c2937edcef63abe378

OPEN ITEMS / FOLLOW-UPS
  - Followup #107 (UNIQUE on districts.name): called out in the PR body and
    in the security-reviewer's single WARN as the structural fix for the
    409 race window. The current pre-flight SELECT is best-effort only; two
    concurrent operator clicks can race-insert duplicates between the SELECT
    and the INSERT. Recommend the #107 migration (case-folded UNIQUE + trim
    decisions) land before this endpoint is exposed to more than one
    operator concurrently, or before any FE binds to it.

  - Followups #73 / #74 (no backend test suite, no backend lint): reiterated
    as the verification-approach drivers this session. Until those land,
    sec/operator/migration PRs lean on node --check + curl smoke as
    evidence. Sibling cluster still open.

  - pg-pool-consolidation followup: all three reviewers independently
    flagged the per-route new pg.Pool() instance as a pattern divergence
    consistent with several other route modules. Folds into the existing
    follow-up; no action this PR.

  - Operator-onboarding next slices (queued, not blocked by anything in
    this PR): (a) POST /api/operator/districts/:id/schools to attach a
    school-tenant under a district; (b) POST /api/operator/districts/:id/
    first-admin to mint the first district_admin + seed user_school_access
    across the district's schools; (c) frontend operator console.

  - Remote branch feat/operator-create-district was NOT deleted from origin
    at session close; repo policy deleteBranchOnMerge is false. Operator
    can delete manually if desired.

NEXT SESSION SHOULD
  Pick up the operator-onboarding work with slice (a): POST /api/operator/
  districts/:id/schools. Same operator-only middleware chain, same
  schema-already-in-prod pattern (tenants.district_id from M028 + tenants
  itself pre-existing). The composite-FK design from M028 makes
  cross-district leakage structurally impossible at the schema layer; the
  endpoint must take district_id from the path param, never from req.body
  — that is the single biggest correctness hazard called out in the prior
  read-only audit.

BRANCH:  sec/auth-left-join-tenants + sec/resolve-target-tenant-null-guard
DISPOSITION: merged (PR #270 @ 953d59a20a72045626106b6c89c168de0d7305a5, PR #271 @ 036cc54539c09bbc1f022ab0a1869861c2719a1b); feat/operator-first-district-admin parked (kept, not discarded)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TASK SUMMARY
  Prep for an operator-only "create a district's first district_admin" endpoint
  (parked). Two prerequisite auth/tenant-isolation fixes were found, built,
  reviewed by the full triad, merged, and deployed:
    1. Auth identity queries: INNER JOIN -> LEFT JOIN so a principal with a null
       home-tenant can authenticate instead of being dropped to a 401.
    2. A null-tenant write guard across the route helpers that bind a write to
       the caller's home-tenant, so a null-tenant principal that omits an
       explicit target is rejected (400) instead of writing an unscoped row.

FILES TOUCHED
  routes/auth.js — PR #270: JOIN -> LEFT JOIN tenants in the login, session-
    hydration, and Google-signin identity queries (3 handlers).
  routes/{students,screener,staffManagement,mtssMeetings,users,prereferralForms,
    disciplineReferrals,student504,csvImport}.js — PR #271: identical null-tenant
    fallback guard added to each file's local target-tenant resolver (9 helpers).

MIGRATIONS RUN
  none (both PRs are application-layer only; no schema change)

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  Both PRs are Section 5 changes; full triad (tenant-isolation primary +
  security + privacy) run on each, all APPROVED.
  PR #270: identity queries stay pinned to a single principal; the join change
    only converts a no-match from 0 rows to 1 null-padded row, never widens a
    set. Downstream scope still flows through the central accessible-tenant
    resolver, which returns an empty set for a null-tenant principal (fails
    closed). No cross-tenant read/write introduced.
  PR #271: closes an unscoped-orphan-write path on 9 write surfaces. Guard fires
    only when the caller has no home-tenant AND no explicit target; a tenant-
    bound caller is byte-for-byte unchanged. Auditor confirmed via an exhaustive
    repo-wide sweep that, apart from one named out-of-scope file, these 9 helpers
    are the complete set of unguarded home-tenant write-binds.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched: none. No new fields stored; no payload widened (one display
    field can now resolve to null for the new principal class -- strictly less
    data).
  Logging audit: no logging added or modified in either PR; new error bodies are
    static strings (no PII, no error.message, no stack).
  Cross-tenant risk: none identified; PR #271 is privacy-positive (prevents
    detaching records from their school). CSV-delete invariant preserved on the
    new reject path.

VERIFICATION
  merge-sha verification: MATCH for PR #270 (head == merge^2) and PR #271
    (head == merge^2); both true 2-parent merge commits.
  misattribution check: PASS (1 FIXES line; cited SHA is a member of the cited
    PR's commit list).
  PR #270 pre-merge: role-sweep regression (every role returns 1 identity row,
    LEFT == INNER, display field non-null) + null-tenant dev-seed HTTP smoke
    (login + hydration return 200, no 401 lockout); both PASS, seed deleted.
  PR #271 pre-merge: dev HTTP smoke via a write route, 3 cases -- null-tenant
    no-target -> 400; null-tenant valid-target -> 201; tenant-bound no-target ->
    201 in own tenant (no regression); all PASS, seeds deleted.

    window health-stability poll: 15/15 samples HTTP 200 over ~4.7 min, no

TASK SUMMARY
  Built and shipped Slice 2 of the bulk staff import: the operator-console
  staff-import COMMIT endpoint. Unlike the Slice 1 dry-run, it provisions staff
  accounts from one CSV per tenant in a single all-or-nothing transaction,
  generates a 7-day password-setup token per row, sends a setup email per
  account, and writes one append-only audit row per account.

FILES TOUCHED
  migration-047-staff-import-audit.sql  — new append-only audit table (one row per provisioned account)
  routes/operatorStaffImport.js         — shared row validator + commit handler (token / email / audit)
  routes/operatorDistricts.js           — register the commit route on the operator router

MIGRATIONS RUN
  migration-047-staff-import-audit.sql — audit table; applied to prod by the
    operator in their own terminal; idempotent (CREATE ... IF NOT EXISTS inside
    one BEGIN/COMMIT).

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW
  Path-bound tenant scope (parent ids from the URL path, never the CSV); two §5
  pre-flights before any write; tenant_id is the path school tenant; the
  parent-scope id column is path-derived only. The tenant-isolation reviewer
  reviewed the same commits on the predecessor PR and returned APPROVED; no
  cross-tenant path introduced.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched: the email, display-name, and role fields are written to
    the users table (the feature's purpose); a setup-credential token is
    generated per account.
  Logging audit: counts/codes only; the token is never logged and never in any
    response body (only inside the setup-link email); the uploaded CSV is
    deleted on every exit path; the audit table stores ids + role only (no
    email, no name, no token).
  Cross-tenant risk: none identified; structural path binding.
  §4B grep (narrow + wide) on this entry's diff vs origin/main: narrow 0; wide 0
    PII (the lone wide hit is a tracked source filename — a dispositioned false
    positive). No real test email, operator address, reviewer name, tenant id,
    or school name in the entry.

VERIFICATION
  npm test — n/a (no backend test suite; followup #73).
  npm run lint — n/a (backend lint not configured; followup #74).
  local commit smoke — 30/30 passed (synthetic, local dev DB, email sender
    disabled): happy-path provisioning + audit rows + token fields, both
    all-or-nothing gates (zero writes), wrong-parent 404, row cap, §4B-narrow
    bodies/errors.
  prod commit smoke — PASS: imported=2; 2 audit rows confirmed via counts-only
    SQL.
  prod deliverability — PASS: 1 synthetic row to a real monitored mailbox,
    delivery confirmed (inbox receipt + provider dashboard "delivered"); the
    setup link works.
  merge-sha verification: PASS — PR #290 merge commit second-parent matches the
    feature tip SHA.

  STACKED-PR MIS-MERGE + RECOVERY
    The feature PR was first opened stacked on the validator-chore branch. The
    chore PR merged to main BEFORE the feature PR merged into its stacked base,
    which orphaned the feature commits from main: the deploy carried the dry-run
    but not the commit route, so prod returned 404 on commit while validate
    returned 200. Diagnosed as a landing gap, not a deploy/service mismatch.
    Recovered by opening a fresh PR (#290) of the identical, already-reviewed
    commit-route commits with base main — merge-tree clean, zero drift, so the
    reviewer triad was NOT re-run. A fresh non-author §2A approval was obtained
    before merge; no self-merge, no admin-override. The re-deploy then carried
    the route; the prod smokes above confirm it live.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TASK SUMMARY
  Read-only diagnosis + remediation planning for a duplicate-tenant / duplicate-
  account mix-up at one real school. Two tenants exist for the same school: a
  legacy standalone tenant (id 10, district_id NULL, 270 synthetic sandbox
  students) and the real tenant (id 19, district_id 10, 330 real students). One
  staff user holds two accounts under one email — user 54 (district_admin, home
  tenant 10) and user 103 (counselor, home tenant 19). Login resolves by email
  only, so the user landed on 54 and saw the synthetic roster. Scope: fix only
  this one user; do not touch tenant 10's students or any other account.

FILES TOUCHED (all untracked, scripts/ops/, no git add)
  2026-06-16-duplicate-tenant-diagnosis.sql — read-only tenant/student/account-
    scope diagnosis (the initial duplicate-tenant audit)
  2026-06-16-dup-account-audit.sql — read-only: state of users 54/103,
    user_school_access rows, dynamic FK-reference scan for user 54
  2026-06-16-dup-account-remediation.sql — txn (manual COMMIT): email-rename
    neutralize on 54 + role->school_admin on 103; tight id+email+tenant_id
    guards; PRE/POST verification; login email is a runtime \set fill-in
  2026-06-16-dup-account-dryrun-dev.sql — dev-only fixture dry-run, single
    BEGIN; ... ROLLBACK; (ran against localhost dev DB; PASSED)
  2026-06-16-dup-account-runbook.md — owner-run prod run-book (review gate →
    conninfo proof → audit → remediation → COMMIT/ROLLBACK checklist →
    live-verify → rollback-after-commit)
  No tracked file changed this session.

MIGRATIONS RUN
  none

DEV DRY-RUN RESULT
  Ran 2026-06-16-dup-account-dryrun-dev.sql against localhost:5432/tiertrak
  (conninfo confirmed localhost, not Render) inside BEGIN; ... ROLLBACK;.
  Fixture mirrored the real shape (scratch district + 2 tenants + 2 users
  sharing one email). Both guarded UPDATEs printed UPDATE 1; post-rename the
  fixture email resolved to exactly one row (the school_admin); wrong-tenant
  guard printed UPDATE 0; post-rollback the fixture email matched 0 rows.
  Dev left unmutated. One schema delta surfaced: dev districts requires
  auth_mode NOT NULL (M034) — added to the fixture only; remediation script
  inserts no district, so unaffected.

TENANT ISOLATION REVIEW (Section 5)
  No code path changed. Remediation is two single-row UPDATEs on users, each
  guarded by id AND email AND tenant_id, confirmed single-row in dev. User 103
  is legacy tenant-scoped (district_id IS NULL) so a role change alone is
  sufficient — no user_school_access write. No cross-tenant/cross-district read
  or write introduced.

PRIVACY REVIEW (Section 4B)
  PII fields touched in artifacts: the affected user's login email is the
  rename target, supplied only as a runtime \set fill-in — never written to
  disk in any committed surface. Diagnosis reports counts and internal IDs only
  (no student/staff names, no emails in this log or the branch name). The
  neutralized sentinel email is non-routable and carries no real PII. No PII
  logged.

    Section 2A review of the remediation script by the designated non-author
    reviewer → connect to prod + prove conninfo is the Render host → run audit →
    confirm (iii) shows non-zero FK refs to user 54 (DELETE unsafe, rename
    correct) and (ii) shows user 103 has no user_school_access rows → fill in
    the login email → run remediation → COMMIT only if both UPDATE 1 + email
    resolves to exactly one row (id 103).

    POST /api/screener-results/reset returned 500 in prod while
    /reset/preview succeeded (returned count 108). Root cause: M049
    (screener_reset_audit) had never been applied to the prod DB, so the
    in-transaction audit INSERT threw pg 42P01 (undefined_table); the
    transaction rolled back correctly (zero rows deleted, no orphaned delete).
    Resolution: operator applied M049 to prod by hand; live reset verified.
    No handler change made or needed — the rollback behaved as designed; the
    fix was operational, not code.

  Systemic cause: TierTrak prod migrations are NOT auto-applied (server.js
  bootstraps only a curated CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS set; no migrate-runner,
  no render.yaml/preDeploy hook). A merged migration can lag its consuming
  code. This is the 2nd occurrence (M035 -> 42703 at S108; now M049 -> 42P01).

  FAST-FOLLOW ORDER (recorded; none started this session, each off fresh main):
    1. migration-apply gate/checklist (TOP PRIORITY — 2nd occurrence)
    2. sec/ dedicated tighter rate limiter on /reset (rollupOperationLimiter-
       style; /reset only, not /reset/preview)
    3. fix/ screener.js commit-route error-shape bug (metaError.error.status
       masks a 400 as 500; re-grep metaError.error — line shifted post-merge)
    4. sec/jwt-algorithm-pin (pin algorithms:['HS256'] in shared requireAuth)

NEXT SESSION SHOULD
  Start the migration-apply gate as follow-up #1 (branch off fresh main). Weigh:
  (a) an idempotent ordered migrate-runner in deploy, (b) an applied-migrations
  table + fail-fast startup assertion that required migrations are present, or
  (c) at minimum a per-PR "apply migration before/with code deploy" checklist
  wired into landing-the-plane. Then the /reset rate limiter, the screener.js
  error-shape fix, and the JWT algorithm pin, in that order.
